## KAYAK's insights & trends for flights to Bali
### Get data-powered insights and trends into flights to Bali to help you find the cheapest flights, the best time to fly and much more.
Prices
Airlines
More
### What is the cheapest flight to Bali?
These are the best return prices found by users searching on KAYAK in the last 72 hours.
#### The cheapest ticket to Bali from India found in the last 72 hours was **to Denpasar, at ₹ 25,392 return**. The most popular route is [Bengaluru (BLR) to Denpasar (DPS)](https://www.kayak.co.in/flight-routes/Bengaluru-Intl-BLR/Denpasar-Bali-Ngurah-Rai-DPS) and the cheapest return airline ticket found on this route in the last 72 hours was ₹ 25,392.
### How much is a flight to Bali?
The average and cheapest price for all round-trip flights to Bali from each origin found by users searching on KAYAK in the last month.
#### A flight to Bali costs **₹ 30,662 on average**. The cheapest price found on KAYAK in the last 2 weeks was ₹ 12,885 for the route Mumbai (BOM) to Denpasar (DPS). The most popular routes on KAYAK are Bengaluru to Denpasar which costs ₹ 42,341 on average, and New Delhi to Denpasar, which costs ₹ 40,069 on average.
##### See prices from:
New Delhi
Bengaluru
Mumbai
### What is the cheapest month to fly from India to Bali?
To calculate monthly average prices, KAYAK takes all prices for each month over the last year for flights from India to Bali, removes the top 0.1% to account for outliers, and then takes the median of all values for each month.
#### The cheapest month for flights from India to Bali is **February** , when tickets cost ₹ 26,968 (return) on average. On the other hand, the most expensive months are December and September, when the average cost of round-trip tickets is ₹ 39,252 and ₹ 39,067 respectively.
### What is the cheapest time of day to fly to Bali?
The average price for all round-trip flights from India to Bali depending on the time of departure, clicked by users on KAYAK in the last 2 weeks.
#### The cheapest time of day to fly to Bali is generally **in the morning** , when retur flights cost ₹ 34,944 on average. The most expensive time of day to fly to Bali is generally at night, which is peak travel time and where the average cost of a ticket is ₹ 56,107.
### How far in advance should I book a flight from India to Bali?
To calculate weekly average prices, KAYAK takes all prices for each week before departure over the last year for round-trip flights to Bali, removes the top 0.1% to account for outliers, and then takes the average of all the values for each week.
#### To get a below-average price on a flight from India to Bali, you should book around **5 weeks before departure** , which saves you about 18% compared to booking last-minute. For the absolute cheapest price, our data suggests you should book 26 weeks before departure.
### Which is the cheapest airport to fly into in Bali?
#### Prices will differ depending on the departure airport, but generally, the cheapest airport to fly to in Bali is **Denpasar Bali Ngurah Rai Airport (DPS)** , with an average flight price of ₹ 20,758.
### Which airlines fly most frequently to Bali?
#### India and Bali are connected by different airlines. The airlines that fly that route the most regularly are **IndiGo** (21 flights per week), **Air India** (10 flights per week), and **Garuda Indonesia** (7 flights per week).
### How long is the flight to Bali?
#### An average direct flight from India to Bali takes **11h 34m** , covering a distance of 8434 km. The shortest route is [**Bengaluru (BLR) to Denpasar (DPS)**](https://www.kayak.co.in/flight-routes/Bengaluru-Intl-BLR/Denpasar-Bali-Ngurah-Rai-DPS) with an average flight time of 6h 55m.
### How many direct flights to Bali are there each day?
#### There are around **6 direct flights** from within India to Bali every day. Most flights (82%) depart at night.
### How many direct flights to Bali are there each week?
#### Each week there are around 38 direct flights from within India to Bali. The most common day for departures is Monday, with 16% of flights taking off on this day.
### How many long-haul flights are there to Bali each week?
#### Each week, there are **10 long-haul flights** (6-12 hour flight duration) and 28 medium-haul flights (3-6 hour flight duration) to Bali. There aren’t any short-haul flights (up to 3 hour flight duration).
### How many cities have direct flights to Bali?
#### From India, there are direct flights to Bali from **4 cities**. The city with the most direct flights is Bengaluru, with 14 direct flights each week.
### What is the most popular destination in Bali?
#### Based on KAYAK flight searches, the most popular destination is [Denpasar](https://www.kayak.co.in/flight-routes/India-IN0/Denpasar-Bali-Ngurah-Rai-DPS) (100% of total searches to Bali).

## FAQs - booking Bali flights
  * ### How does KAYAK find such low prices on flights from India to Bali?
#### KAYAK is a travel search engine. That means we look across the web to find the best prices we can find for our users. With over 2 billion flight queries processed yearly, we are able to display a variety of prices and options on flights from India to Bali.
  * ### How does KAYAK's flight Price Forecast tool help me choose the right time to buy my flight ticket from India to Bali?
#### KAYAK’s flight Price Forecast tool uses historical data to determine whether the price for a flight from India to Bali is likely to change within 7 days, so travellers know whether to wait or book now.
  * ### What is the Hacker Fare option on flights from India to Bali?
#### Hacker Fares allow you to combine one-way tickets in order to save you money over a traditional return ticket. You could then fly from India to Bali with an airline and back with another airline.
  * ### What is KAYAK's "flexible dates" feature and why should I care when looking for a flight from India to Bali?
#### Sometimes travel dates aren't set in stone. If your preferred travel dates have some wiggle room, flexible dates will show you all the options when flying from India to Bali up to 3 days before/after your preferred dates. You can then pick the flights that suit you best.
  * ### What is the cheapest day to fly to Bali?
#### Based on KAYAK data, the cheapest day to fly to Bali is **Friday** when return tickets can be as cheap as ₹ 46,386. On the other hand, the most expensive day to fly is Thursday, when return prices are ₹ 51,045 on average.

Flight delayed for one hour last minute change the gate this is something new? Wak all over the airport bit fast bording to flight smd miss my meeting in kuaa lumpur is the year end meeting no internet in flught which is not good Anyway overall is good thanks to the crew on duty Un able to check in on line and choose the seat thatbi wanted and in flight meal too unable to purchase rrally sad Should get priority seat in next booking
Flight was delayed from 8pm to near midnight. They made us board then sit in the cabin and wait for even longer for take-off. No compensation whatsoever - not even in a free drink or meal
Fast Pass but delay at Check-In Counter. Limited Counter when there are alot of ppls Check-In
We were told that flight was delayed by 20 mins, but it left close to an hour late. But they did make it up and arrived early; it's just very difficult to keep waiting when it's a late flight. Food was mediocre.
The flight has been delayed for nearly an hour, yet neither the captain nor the chief steward made an effort to explain the reason for the delay or offer any apologies to the passengers.
We recently flew with AirAsia and encountered several issues. Our flight was delayed by over an hour, and both boarding and takeoff were postponed without any prior notification. Although we eventually realized there was a delay on our own, the crew did inform us afterward. To make up for lost time, the pilot took off at high speed, resulting in some turbulent moments that were quite scary. Despite these challenges, the cabin crew did an excellent job managing the situation and easing the inconvenience. Overall, while the delays and turbulence made for a stressful experience, I appreciated the crew’s efforts to keep us as comfortable as possible.
I had a wonderful experience with this company. The staff were all very friendly and helpful. Our pilot was skilled and clearly experienced. The value for the price was excellent. I would definitely recommend them.
Very quick turn around, plane was in good condition, alles paletti.
Flight was delayed 110 mins.. Crew on Ground or in Air had no regrets.. No apologies... Very poor boarding process... Awful food and beverage choice.
Flight was delayed and due to this we almost miss the connection flight
The flight was delayed by 3 hours, causing me to miss my connecting international flight. I then had to wait in the airport 18 hours for the next flight. The reason for the delay was a computer issue, not something outside of their control like weather. To add insult to injury, instead of trying to expedite the flight to make up for lost time, they ran a carry-on baggage check to verify every bag onboard belonged to someone on the plane, which took another 20 minutes.
झूठा है इंडिगो फ्लाइट में मील दिया नहीं जब शिकायत किया तो बोला आपको मिल चुका है
It took a lot of time to declare that the flight gonna be cancelled ☹️ very disappointed.
They denied my meal which was included in ticket and also damaged my bag completely and broke the wheel and still not accepting their fault
As budget airline everything was good. Except that - boarding process can be more organised. Then if entertainment systems are not operational. Its better to keep them switch off.
Flight cancelled after 4+ hour delay. Indigo created a nightmare scenario and showed the most callus attitude towards the passengers I will refer to them was IndiNoGo!
Very nice and pleasant. Boarding was quick and the crew were responsive
Foreign nationals are not permitted to check in online in advance. We can only check in at the airport, so choice of seat was limited. I was stuck in a very uncomfortable location. I would expect a meal included in a flight of this duration. The food options to buy are very basic. IngiGo are a very good domestic carrier, but if they are serious about operating more international, longer haul flights, they need to improve this service to match every other airline I know.
Terrible customer service. My last choice to fly now if there is no other options.
For budget airline, it’s not bad at all. It’s definitely value for money. I added on the priority check in and eSIM. It’s well worth it.
First time flying with VietJet Air is so so. Check-in process for domestic is okay and easy but during our flight back to Manila, the ground staff at Tan Son Nhat International Airport weighed our hand luggage before boarding when in fact, it should be weighed during the check-in process. They are very strict in terms of the 7kg hand carry it’s either you’ll pay extra, or leave things behind.
I was unable to check in online so had to line up at the airport. When I got to the counter? The staff told me I had to check my carry on luggage, which cost $70. When I boarded the plane, I saw many suitcases that were bigger and heavier than my carry on… so not sure how they were able to bring them on and I wasn’t …
Waited for 1 hour on tarmac because they couldn’t locate a tow truck. Flight was fine once we eventually took off.
Late departure, late arrival, and personal items on laps during takeoff and landing. Departure gate opened on time, but no direction as to whether to board at front or rear of plane based on seat number. Fashionable staff with sunglasses inside the plane and cockpit remained for ten minutes after the boarding complete announcement. For a scheduled 80 minute flight, adjusted to 65 minute as noted on takeoff; the total time on the plane with delays was r more than two hours. No confidence had there been an emergency that anyone would have been able or aware of what to do.
The flight was OK, but you need to warn passengers going to Vietnam that the full first, middle and last name needs to be in the issued visa, At least 5 passengers on that pane did not have that. We all had to pay $298 each to some agent to amend the visa. At least one lady could not and was left behind.
VietJet is a discount airline like EasyJet, Flair or Spirit. I flew 3 flights in 2 weeks and all were Airbus 321's. 2 of the flights left and arrived on time and 1 left and arrived about 45minutes late. Seat pitch in economy is 29" but exit row seats have super-generous leg room. There are no power ports or entertainment of any kind. Food is best purchased on-line before the flight but there is a limited selection available in flight - cash only. The aircraft are clean and the airline is run as well as any other airline that specializes in economy passengers.
Our flight was 3 hours delayed and the airport was hectic as our boarding pass said one gate while the digital screens said another gate. Attendants were clueless and said to watch both! Vietjwt cared more about selling their merch than even providing any water for the 2 hour flight
Cheap flight but more expensive when the airlines demands you pay over 30$ for 1 kilogram overweight ( it is 8,2kg I have for carry on baggage) The flights announcement 2PM, and it departed 4 PM, no announcement for delaying, People says Vietjet is delayJet airlines in their blood.
Terrible airline... Originally, the flight was canceled and didn't receive a notification, had to learn from their twitter account... Rebooking was a huge pain... Every other airline like American and United did eventually fly a day later... The flight had a layover, no other airline needed one... India is such a weak nation globally, this airline has no leverage to negotiate good airspace... Flight left late, again no notifications as to why at the airport. On the plane, wifi is spotty, chargers don't work... The only saving grace used to be good food. Even that has gone down the drain. Just the worst!
The web check in doesn’t work. Had to wait 10 minutes on the counter for them to figure out boarding. I understand AI Express doesn’t have a practice to offer food to everyone in the business class. The flight attendant asks for name (not sure why she doesn’t know it especially with only 8 passengers in the business class) to figure out whether or not they should serve food. KAYAK should sell AI Express tickets separately, not as part of AI. Neither the experience close to AI, the Maharaja points also don’t get awarded despite Express being part of AI.
Never ever travel with Air India is a very conscious promise made to myself. Poor customer service , irresponsible staff, pathetic operations and bad planes. Overall -0/10. Planes had mosquitoes in it.. i am still thinking what international standards of hygiene are they even following????. Operations team has no quality standards. They can route you anywhere on any plane inspite of a confirmed ticket and a web checkin… without your consent. Go for AirIndia if you love challenges and roller coasters and safety, time and hygiene is never your priority.
tray table was inclined by 30 degress so could not use it
The plane was very dated with the worst entertainment system i have seen in 30 years. Flight attendants were responsive.
I think if i find different options I’ll travel with it but not with air India they never loaded the luggage’s and they announce the luggage’s will be arriving on belt number 5 when all passengers reach to the belt we find they haven’t loaded the luggage’s
The flight was on time, the entire experience was very smooth. The Flight crew were incredibly professional and nice. Made us feel very comfortable
They rescheduled flight..no customer care to even reach or accommodate
Unnecessary delays & Gate changes, so much inconvenience after 15-16 hours flight
Old and filthy aircraft Boarding was free for all with no zones
Singapore Airlines8.2AirAsia6.8IndiGo6.8VietJet Air5.6Air India5.5

## How to get the cheapest flight ticket from India to Bali
Look no further. We've gone through all the searches for this route on momondo so you have the important information and insights to find the cheapest flight ticket for your trip
Prices
Airlines
Other information
### What is the cheapest month to fly to Bali?
If you’re not able to fly to Bali in January, then February is your next best option. Flight prices from India to Bali during February average around ₹ 35,003.
### What is the cheapest day to depart on your flight to Bali?
On average, the least expensive day to fly to Bali from India is on a Saturday. momondo users have found tickets for Saturday departures for as low as ₹ 46,548. However, this price may fluctuate depending on the airline, the season, and holidays. On the other hand, Thursday is the most expensive day to depart from India because it is more convenient for both business and leisure travellers. As a result, prices tend to be around ₹ 56,060.
### What is the cheapest time of day to depart on your flight to Bali?
Our data shows that the cheapest India to Bali flights are usually those with departure times in the morning. Our users have found flights to Bali in the morning for as low as ₹ 34,944. Flights in the evening are often more expensive, with an average ticket price of ₹ 56,107.
### What is the cheapest India to Bali flight route?
Fly from Mumbai Chhatrapati Shivaji Intl to Denpasar Bali Ngurah Rai for the best India - Bali flight prices
### How far in advance should I book a flight to Bali?
You are more likely to see cheaper flights to Bali from India when booking weeks ahead of your departure date. In general, you can expect to find flights to Bali for around ₹ 38,866 when searching weeks before you fly. However, flight prices might be lower outside of this timeframe. For example, people recently found flights to Bali from India for as low as ₹ 35,003 on momondo.
### What cabin class options are available on India to Bali flights?
There are 3 cabin classes available to choose from on India to Bali flights. Cabin price and availability will vary by airline so perform a search above to find the right deals for you.
### Can stopping save me money on India to Bali flights?
You could potentially save up to 39% on your flight if you consider stops on a India to Bali flight.
### What is the cheapest airline that flies from India to Bali?
AirAsia, Scoot, and VietJet Air are the most popular carriers operating from India to Bali
### Which airlines fly to Bali?
VietJet Air, Singapore Airlines and IndiGo are the top three most popular airlines that fly to Bali from India according to our data. VietJet Air is the first choice among our users, with 34% of people opting to fly with this airline when travelling to Bali. Keep in mind that there are 15 different airlines that fly to Bali on a routine basis, so you have a few additional options to choose from.
### How many flights depart from India to Bali on average per day?
5 flights are operational daily from India to Bali as of March 2026. The most popular departure time for this route is 00:00, with 72% of flights per day taking off in the morning.
### How many flights depart from India to Bali on average per week?
As of March 2026, there are 40 flights per week flying from India to Bali. The most common day of the week flights depart is Mon, with 18% of flights taking off on this day.
### How long is the flight from India to Bali?
Flight times vary depending on what airport in India you fly out of and what airport in Bali you fly into. For example, flying from Bengaluru to Denpasar takes 6h 55m on average. On the other hand, flying from New Delhi to Denpasar takes 8h 05m on average. Keep in mind that it’s always quicker to opt for a non-stop flight, so be sure to check momondo to see if there are any available for your route.

It is recommended that you verify with the airline you are booking with for travel from India to Bali.

The required travel and health documents for your Bali trip may vary depending on your itinerary and personal circumstances. ## Fly to Bali from India: flight guide
Planning a trip to Bali and in search of affordable flight tickets to Bali? Simply enter your travel dates above to start your search for the best airfares. Additionally, continue reading to discover more about your journey, available airlines, flight schedules, tips for finding cheap tickets, and much more.
### Flight tickets to Bali
Airfares to Bali are subject to frequent changes. These prices are influenced by multiple factors, including demand, travel dates and times, seat availability, and the pricing strategies adopted by airlines. Furthermore, the selection of a specific travel class, such as first class, business class, or economy class, can further affect the airfare rates.
### How to find cheap flight tickets to Bali
Getting the cheapest flights to Bali requires a combination of flexibility, perseverance, and a dash of luck. Here are some valuable tips to help you find the best prices: 
  * 1. Stay open to adjusting your travel dates and times, as airfares tend to be cheaper during early mornings, late evenings, and weekdays.
  * 2. Explore alternative airports as they may offer more affordable options.
  * 3. Consider booking separate one-way tickets for your outbound and inbound flights, as this could potentially be cheaper than a round-trip ticket.
  * 4. Opt for connecting flights to Bali to potentially save on costs.
  * 5. Keep an eye out for last-minute flight deals to Bali.


### Airlines that offer cheap flights to Bali
Looking to fly from India to Bali? Keep in mind that direct flights are not available. However, there's no need to worry as you can still opt for an airline that offers convenient connection flights. Detailed information about various airlines and their flight options can be found on our booking page.
### Everything you need to know about Bali airport
Ngurah Rai International Airport, known as Bali Airport, is the primary entry point to the beautiful island of Bali, Indonesia. It serves as a crucial hub for both domestic and international flights, catering to tourists worldwide. With a modern single terminal, the airport offers contemporary amenities and services. Located conveniently in Tuban, Kuta, Bali Airport enjoys excellent connectivity to different parts of the island. Passengers have easy access to the airport through various public transportation options, such as taxis, ride-sharing services, and airport shuttles. Car rental services are also available for those who prefer independent exploration. 
### Book flights to Bali on Trip.com and unlock extra benefits
Trip.com doesn't stop at finding cheap airfares to Bali. We strive to enhance your travel experience by providing a multitude of extra perks and benefits. By making your flight reservation with us, you gain access to exclusive discounts on hotels, car rentals, and airport transfers, ensuring maximum savings on your trip. As a token of appreciation, Trip.com rewards you with Tripcoins, our exclusive loyalty program currency that can be utilized for future travel endeavors.

## Frequently asked questions: Booking Bali flights
  * ### What is the average flight ticket price from India to Bali?
The average ticket price to Bali is ₹ 29,869. Please use this price as a reference only. 
  * ### How long is the flights to Bali?
    * Approx. 10 hours 59 minutes to fly from New Delhi to Bali.
    * Approx. 3 hours 17 minutes to fly from Kuala Lumpur to Bali.
    * Approx. 11 hours 19 minutes to fly from Mumbai to Bali.
    * Approx. 12 hours 27 minutes to fly from Ahmedabad to Bali.
  * ### How to get cheapest flights to Bali?
You can book cheaper flights in the following ways: 
    * Search for tickets to a city or airport near Bali.
    * Look up different airlines that have flights to Bali.
    * Browse different departure dates from the same month.
    * Filter for different departure times from the same day.
    * Check for discount information on various social media sites.
    * Explore deals and discounts on airline websites.
  * ### What is the most popular month to visit Bali?
Understanding peak and off travel seasons to Bali can help you arrange your travel plans accordingly. The most popular month to visit Bali is April. The average price of flight tickets to Bali in April is ₹ 22,117. 
  * ### When are flight to Bali cheapest?
Flight tickets to Bali are the cheapest in June compared to other months, the average price is ₹ 19,166. 
  * ### Which popular airlines have flights to Bali?
Popular airlines with flights to Bali: [ Indonesia AirAsia](https://in.trip.com/flights/airline-qz/pt-indonesia-airasia/), [ Batik Air Malaysia](https://in.trip.com/flights/airline-od/batik-air-malaysia/), and [ Lion Air](https://in.trip.com/flights/airline-jt/lion-air/).

# Fly to/from Bali with fares   
starting at INR 20,599*
Fly to/from Bali with fares starting at INR 20,599*  
  
  
  

Fly to/from Bali with fares starting at INR 20,599*  
  
  
  
  

### **Offer Details:**
Dreaming of a tropical escape? Let Bali amaze you with its pristine beaches, lush landscapes and an enchanting culture.
Now fly daily non-stop between Delhi and Bali, with fares starting at INR 20,599*.
  
Please refer to the table below for the starting one-way and return fares: 
Route |  Cabin |  Currency |  Starting all-inclusive one-way fares* |  Starting all-inclusive return fares*  
---|---|---|---|---  
**India-Bali** |  Economy Class |  INR |  20,599 |  39,999  
Premium Economy |  INR |  30,999 |  59,999  
Business Class |  INR |  61,199 |  1,19,999  
**Bali-India** |  Economy Class |  IDR |  38,27,000 |  74,32,000  
Premium Economy |  IDR |  57,60,000 |  1,11,47,000  
Business Class |  IDR |  1,13,70,000 |  2,22,94,000  
Flight schedule is subject to regulatory approvals.
Also, connecting Bali from Mumbai, Ahmedabad, Bengaluru, Hyderabad, Chennai, Kolkata, Kochi, and Amritsar via Delhi.
Look forward to a comfortable inflight experience with spacious seating, warm meals, and quintessential Indian hospitality.

## About Bali
Bali is an Indonesian island known for its forested volcanic mountains, iconic rice paddies, beaches and coral reefs. The island is home to religious sites such as cliffside Uluwatu Temple. To the south, the beachside city of Kuta has lively bars, while Seminyak, Sanur and Nusa Dua are popular resort towns. The island is also known for its yoga and meditation retreats.
## Frequently asked questions about flying to Bali
What is the best airline to fly to Bali?
What counts as the best airline for your flights to Bali really depends on your individual needs. You may have a favorite carrier or a loyalty program you like, but price, convenience, and airports served also come into play. Flexibility and booking terms – Can you change flights if you want to stay longer or get a refund if you have to postpone your trip? – are also factors that help determine which airline is best to fly to Bali.  
  
In terms of direct flights, Indonesia AirAsia is the most popular, flying directly from 12 cities. Indonesia AirAsia flies to I Gusti Ngurah Rai International Airport (DPS).  
  
Other popular airlines flying to Bali are Lion, Garuda Indonesia, Batik Air, Jetstar, Wings Abadi Airlines.
  * Lion flies directly from 11 cities. Lion flies to I Gusti Ngurah Rai International Airport (DPS).
  * Garuda Indonesia flies directly from 11 cities. Garuda Indonesia flies to I Gusti Ngurah Rai International Airport (DPS).
  * Batik Air flies directly from 10 cities. Batik Air flies to I Gusti Ngurah Rai International Airport (DPS).
  * Jetstar flies directly from 9 cities. Jetstar flies to I Gusti Ngurah Rai International Airport (DPS).
  * Wings Abadi Airlines flies directly from 5 cities. Wings Abadi Airlines flies to I Gusti Ngurah Rai International Airport (DPS).


Which cities have direct flights to Bali?
  * Indonesia AirAsia offers direct flights to Bali from 12 cities. The most frequent departures are: Jakarta, Perth, Singapore, Labuan Bajo and Federal Territory of Kuala Lumpur.
  * Lion offers direct flights to Bali from 11 cities. The most frequent departures are: Makassar, Surabaya, Yogyakarta, Kupang and Semarang.
  * Garuda Indonesia offers direct flights to Bali from 11 cities. The most frequent departures are: Jakarta, Sydney, Tokyo, Yogyakarta and Melbourne.
  * Batik Air offers direct flights to Bali from 10 cities. The most frequent departures are: Labuan Bajo, Federal Territory of Kuala Lumpur, Perth, Bangkok and Makassar.
  * Jetstar offers direct flights to Bali from 9 cities. The most frequent departures are: Melbourne, Perth, Sydney, Adelaide and Brisbane.
  * Wings Abadi Airlines offers direct flights to Bali from 5 cities. The most frequent departures are: Praya, Kota Tambolaka, Waingapu City, Sumbawa Besar and Bima.


When should you fly to Bali, Indonesia?
Peak travel periods include Jul–Aug and mid-Dec–early Jan. The tropical climate is hot and humid year-round, extremely humid during the rainy season (Oct–Mar) and less so in the dry season (Apr–Sep). Balinese New Year, or "Nyepi", (dates vary) is observed with 24 hours of silence and fasting. The religious festivals of Galungan and Kalingang (dates vary) are celebrated with dancing, feasts and elaborately decorated bamboo poles. The Bali Arts Festival (Jun) has traditional dances, music and night markets.
What is the best airport to fly into Bali?
Bali is served by only one major airport: I Gusti Ngurah Rai International Airport, 46 km away from the city center.
What are cheap flight options for flying from India to Bali?
If you are looking to book airline tickets from India to Bali you have options. Some of the cheapest flights from popular cities include:
  * Batik Air flight from New Delhi, starting at ₹32,452 from Apr 20 to Apr 29
  * Batik Air flight from Mumbai, starting at ₹33,017 from May 29 to Jun 4
  * Batik Air flight from Bengaluru, starting at ₹28,293 from Jun 4 to Jun 10
  * Vietjet flight from Hyderabad, starting at ₹31,980 from Jun 4 to Jun 11
  * Scoot flight from Chennai, starting at ₹24,644 from Apr 12 to Apr 18
  * Thai AirAsia flight from Kolkata, starting at ₹29,907 from May 7 to May 13


What is the cheapest flight to Bali from India?
Of all the flights departing from popular cities in India, the cheapest round-trip flight to Bali starts at ₹24,644 and departs from Chennai on Apr 12 to Apr 18.
How can I find last-minute flight deals to Bali?
Finding last-minute flights to Bali is easy on Google Flights.  
Select your departure city and use the calendar to pick your preferred travel dates and find the lowest ticket prices available. You can even check for flights departing today.  
To find the cheapest tickets, it’s usually best to book at least a few weeks in advance for domestic flights and a few months in advance for cheap international flights.
How can I find flights deals to Bali?
You can find great flight deals to Bali on Google Flights.  
Just enter your departure city, choose Bali in the destination field, and click **Search**.  
You can filter the results to see only nonstop flights or flights under a certain price to more easily plan your perfect budget trip.  
  
You can also turn on price tracking to get alerts if the price changes for a route or flight.

### About Chennai (MAA)
Chennai formally known as Madras is known as the Gateway to the South. Due to its cultural and traditional values, Chennai is the heart of Tamil Culture. Being the capital city of Indian state of Tamil Nadu, Chennai is also known as the fourth largest city of India. The city is a perfect example of deep rooted culture and heritage. The long standing coconut palms along the streets, beautiful beaches, and warm hospitality show the real colours of this fascinating city. Long stretched gold-sand beaches as well as lush green fields offer great peace to mind. It is an immense pleasure to spend an evening during the time of sunset with your beloved here. It will be an unforgettable moment of your life while moving at the shore of Chennai beaches holding the hands of your partner. It is quite a lively city and, therefore it lures not just the Indian travellers but also the people across the world. To enjoy your holidays in a grand way, Chennai has many tourists’ attractions sites. There are some prominent places like Fort St. George, The Marina Beach, Santhome Cathedral Basilica, Vivekananda House and Museum, Kapaleeshwara Temple, and Fort Museum which will give you a deeper picture of this metropolis. All these attractions will surely give you a time of your life. Those who like to spend time with animals, can go to Guindy National Park and Snake Park where they can find various species of animals and birds. If you are in this region, then don't miss the chance to enjoy the festive season. The most famous festival of Chennai is 'pongal', celebrated with great zeal and gusto. Though the climate of the city remains hot during all the months but still the winter season is considered as the best time to visit here. The best thing about the city's climate is that it is less humid. To enjoy a comfortable and pleasant trip, just log onto [MakeMyTrip.com](http://www.makemytrip.com/international-flights/) where you will get some attractive discounts on your every booking. From flight tickets to hotel booking, this online travel portal offers you every tour related services to make your holidays more enjoyable. With following few simple steps, you can book your entire tour package here with great ease.

## Flying from India to Bali in 2026
![holding an indian passport in a flight from india to bali](https://www.baliholidaysecrets.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/03/inside-a-flight-from-india-to-bali.webp) ![](https://www.baliholidaysecrets.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/03/arriving-at-bali-airport-from-a-flight-from-india.webp)
Direct flights from India to Bali currently depart from **Delhi, Mumbai, and Bengaluru**. Non-stop flights usually take around **7 to 9 hours** depending on the route.
If you take a connecting flight, most Indian travellers transit through **Singapore or Kuala Lumpur** , which can bring the total time to around **11 to 16 hours** depending on the layover.
For direct routes, **Air India and IndiGo** tend to be the best options. Many flights depart late at night and land in Bali the next morning or early afternoon.
### Quick Facts: India to Bali Flights
  * **Flight Duration:** 7 hrs 30 mins to 9 hrs 15 mins (Direct)
  * **Flight Distance:** Roughly 5,800 km (from Delhi)
  * **Flight Frequency:** 3–4 direct flights daily 
  * **Departure Airports:** DEL, BOM, BLR.
  * **Arrival Airport:** Ngurah Rai International Airport (DPS)
  * **Top Airlines:** Air India, IndiGo, Singapore Airlines, AirAsia, Vietjet
  * **Time Difference:** Bali is **2.5 hrs ahead** of India (IST).


👉 If you’re still planning your trip, you can also check my **[Complete Bali Travel Guide for Indians](https://www.baliholidaysecrets.com/bali-for-indians/)** , where I explain everything from what to do, where to stay, and what to know before arriving.

## How Long are Flights from India to Bali?
![flightradar map showing a flight time and distance from india to bali](https://www.baliholidaysecrets.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/03/flight-time-and-distance-from-india-to-bali.webp)
The total travel time for flights from India to Bali in 2026 depends on whether you take a direct flight or a connecting route. 
For most Indian travellers, the difference is not just the hours in the air but **how tired you feel when you arrive.**
If you land fresh in the morning, you can check into your hotel, grab lunch, and still have energy for your [first time in Bali](https://www.baliholidaysecrets.com/first-time-in-bali/) with a sunset and a cocktail.
### 1. Direct Flights (The Time-Savers)
Direct flights from India to Bali are the easiest option. You skip the hassle of a transit airport and usually arrive much quicker.
  * **From Bengaluru (BLR):** The fastest route, taking about **6 hours 50 minutes**.
  * **From Delhi (DEL):** Usually **7 hours 30 minutes to 8 hours**.
  * **From Mumbai (BOM):** Around **8 hours to 8 hours 30 minutes**.


### 2. Connecting Flights (The Scenic Routes)
If you are not flying from a major hub or prefer a premium airline, you will usually have a connection. These routes are still very common for flights from India to Bali.
  * **Via Singapore (SIN):** Total travel time is typically **10 to 12 hours**. Many travellers choose this route because Changi Airport is excellent for layovers.
  * **Via Kuala Lumpur (KUL):** Usually **9 to 11 hours** total travel time.
  * **Via Bangkok (BKK/DMK):** Generally **10 to 13 hours** depending on the connection.


### Route Comparison at a Glance
**Route Type** | **Departure City** | **Average Total Time** | **Primary Airlines**  
---|---|---|---  
**Direct** | Bengaluru | 6h 50m | IndiGo  
**Direct** | Delhi | 7h 45m | Air India, IndiGo  
**Direct** | Mumbai | 8h 40m | IndiGo  
**Connecting** | Any Major City | 10h – 15h | Singapore Airlines, Malaysia Airlines, Thai Airways  
💡 **Pro Tip:** Most direct flights from India to Bali are overnight red-eye. They leave around midnight and land in Bali between 8:00 AM and 10:00 AM. This works if you want to start your holiday right away.

## Airlines Operating from India to Bali
![airline flights operating from india to bali](https://www.baliholidaysecrets.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/03/airline-flights-operating-from-india-to-bali.webp)
The good news is that flights from India to Bali are much easier to find in 2026 than they were a few years ago. 
Several airlines now connect major Indian cities to Bali either with direct flights or short stopovers.
For you as an Indian traveller, the choice comes down to two simple options. **Direct flight for speed** , or **premium airline with a short transit** for a more comfortable journey.
### India to Bali Flight Schedule & Details
**Airline** | **Flight Type** | **Primary Indian Hubs** | **Best For**  
---|---|---|---  
**Air India** | Direct | Delhi (DEL) | Families & comfort-seekers (Full meal service included)  
**IndiGo** | Direct | Mumbai, Bengaluru, Delhi | Efficiency & travelers on a fixed schedule  
**Singapore Airlines** | Connecting (via SIN) | Major Metros | Luxury, honeymooners, and world-class transit shopping  
**Thai Airways** | Connecting (via BKK) | Major Metros | Travelers who want a short layover & great food  
**Malaysia Airlines** | Connecting (via KUL) | Major Metros | Competitive pricing on full-service tickets  
**VietJet Air** | Connecting (via SGN) | Major Metros | Tourists looking for the lowest premium-tier fares  
### Which Airline Should You Choose?
#### 1. The Direct Route (Air India & IndiGo)
If your goal is simple, get to Bali as fast as possible. Direct flights from India to Bali with Air India or IndiGo save you several hours compared to connecting routes.
  * **Air India:** Since the merger with Vistara, Air India now uses the modern **787-9 Dreamliner** on the Delhi route. It features a true 3-class configuration (Business, Premium Economy, and Economy) and includes hot Indian meals.
  * **IndiGo:** This is the “no-frills” champion. It’s perfect for a 6–8 hour hop if you don’t mind paying for snacks or bringing your own entertainment.


#### 2. The Full-Service Transit (Singapore Airlines)
Some Indian travellers actually prefer breaking the journey with a short stop in Singapore. This splits the trip into two manageable flights and gives you access to **Singapore Changi Airport**.
It is considered one of the best airports in the world and the route is especially popular with honeymooners or travellers who want the journey itself to feel a bit more premium.
#### 3. The Budget Connecting Choice (VietJet or AirAsia)
If price is your top priority, you will often find cheaper flights from India to Bali with connections through Kuala Lumpur or Ho Chi Minh City.
Just keep an eye on the layover time. A cheap ticket can quickly become exhausting if the total travel time stretches to 14 or 15 hours.

## Ticket Prices from India to Bali
![iphone displaying ticket prices from india to bali ](https://www.baliholidaysecrets.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/03/ticket-prices-for-flights-from-india-to-bali.webp)
The cost of flights from India to Bali mainly depends on when you book and whether you choose a direct or connecting route. 
In 2026, prices are fairly competitive, especially with more non-stop flights now operating between India and Bali.
As an Indian traveller, it becomes a simple trade-off between **saving money with a longer route** or **saving time with a direct flight**.
**Class of Travel** | **Typical Price (Off-Peak)** | **Typical Price (Peak Season)**  
---|---|---  
**Economy Class** | ₹32,000 – ₹42,000 | ₹55,000 – ₹85,000  
**Premium Economy** | ₹65,000 – ₹80,000 | ₹95,000+  
**Business Class** | ₹1,10,000 – ₹1,50,000 | ₹1,80,000+  
### Price Breakdown by Departure City
Because several direct flights from India to Bali now depart from major hubs, your departure city can make a noticeable difference to the final ticket price.
  * **From Bengaluru (BLR)**  
Usually the cheapest departure point for direct flights. Round-trip fares with IndiGo often start around **₹35,000**.
  * **From Delhi (DEL)**  
Direct flights on Air India or IndiGo typically range between **₹36,000 and ₹48,000** for economy.
  * **From Mumbai (BOM)**  
Expect prices between **₹34,000 and ₹46,000** for a direct flight.


### Why the Price Varies
  * **Direct vs Connecting**  
Direct flights from India to Bali are often priced surprisingly close to connecting routes. You might save ₹3,000 by choosing a longer transit flight, but many travellers prefer the shorter journey.
  * **Seasonality (Tourist Rush):**
    * **Peak Prices:** May, June, August, and late December.
    * **Best Deals:** February, September, and November.
  * **Full-Service vs Budget Airlines:**   
Airlines like **Air India** usually include meals and checked baggage. With **IndiGo** , you may pay extra for these, which can add ₹4,000–₹6,000 to the real cost of the ticket.


⚠️ **Heads Up:** Indigo seats tend to be…on the thin side. If you’re not comfortable with flying, go with Air India instead. Sure it cost a bit more, but 7-9 hours on a plane with paper thin seats can be a pain.

## Best Indian Flight Booking Platforms
![finding flights from india to bali on makemytrip](https://www.baliholidaysecrets.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/03/best-booking-platforms-for-flights-from-india-to-bali-1170x665.webp)
When booking your flights from India to Bali, where you buy the ticket can matter almost as much as the airline itself. 
Many Indian flight booking platforms often include perks like instant refunds, credit card rewards, or special airline deals.
For most Indian travellers, these are the platforms that tend to offer the **best mix of price, reliability, and customer support.**
**Platform** | **Best Feature** | **Trust Factor** | **Recommended For**  
---|---|---|---  
**[MakeMyTrip](https://www.makemytrip.com/)** | Instant Refunds | High | Families & First-time Bali travelers  
**[Tata Neu](https://www.tataneu.com/travel/home?lob=flight)** | 5% NeuCoins | High | Tata/HDFC Cardholders  
**[EaseMyTrip](https://www.easemytrip.com/)** | Zero Convenience Fee | Medium | Budget-conscious tourists  
**[SkyScanner](http://skyscanner.com/)** | Global Price Search | N/A | Researching the cheapest dates  
### 1. MakeMyTrip (MMT)
MakeMyTrip is**one of the most widely used platforms** in India for international travel. Many Indians book flights from India to Bali here because the interface is simple and the refund options are clear.
  * **Best For:** All-in-one convenience and “Zero Cancellation” add-ons.
  * **Pro Tip:** Look for “MMT Exclusive” deals on Air India and IndiGo which often include free seat selection or meal vouchers.


### 2. Tata Neu
If you already use a Tata Neu credit card, this platform can be surprisingly valuable. The rewards system lets you earn **NeuCoins** which can be used on future travel or shopping.
  * **Best For:** Earning **NeuCoins** (usually 5% back).
  * **The Perk:** You can use your NeuCoins to pay for your next hotel stay in Bali or even shop at Westside/Croma when you get back.


### 3. EaseMyTrip
EaseMyTrip became popular in India because it usually **does not charge a convenience fee** , which can make a noticeable difference on international tickets.
  * **Best For:** Seeing the “real” price upfront without hidden taxes at the final payment screen.
  * **Pro Tip:** If you aren’t using a specific credit card for points, the lack of convenience fees makes this the cheapest direct booking site.


### 4. Skyscanner / Google Flights
These are not booking platforms themselves but **flight search engines**. They are useful for comparing prices across airlines and travel sites before you decide where to book.
  * **Best For:** Comparing prices across all airlines and OTAs (Online Travel Agencies).
  * **The Strategy:** Use [Google Flights](https://www.google.com/travel/flights) to find the best dates and then head to the **Airline’s Official Website** to book. Booking directly with the airline (like AirIndia.com) is always the safest way to handle date changes or refunds.


⚠️ **Warning for Indian Travellers:** Avoid unfamiliar ultra-cheap third-party websites. Saving a little money is not worth the headache if your flight changes and the company has no proper customer support.
* * *
## Finding Cheap India to Bali Flights
Finding cheap flights from India to Bali is not just about picking the lowest number you see on Google. A lot of “cheap” tickets become expensive once baggage, meals, and seat selection get added.
Here are some simply tricks (based on experience) that usually make the biggest difference when booking.
  * **Book 40 to 60 days in advance**  
This is usually the sweet spot for flights from India to Bali. Prices often jump sharply if you wait until the last two weeks.
  * **Search in incognito mode**  
Use a private browser window when checking prices. Some flight search engines track repeated searches and slowly push prices higher.
  * **Use Google Flights or Skyscanner first**  
These tools help you compare dates and airlines quickly. Sometimes moving your trip by one day can save **₹5,000–₹10,000**.
  * **Look for Indian credit card offers**  
Platforms like MakeMyTrip and Ixigo often run discounts with **ICICI, HDFC, or SBI cards** , sometimes cutting **₹3,000–₹5,000** off the ticket.
  * **Check baggage before booking budget airlines**  
A cheaper IndiGo or AirAsia ticket can become expensive once you add baggage and meals. Sometimes a full-service airline ends up costing the same.
  * **Avoid Friday and Saturday departures**  
These are usually the most expensive days to fly. Tuesday and Wednesday flights are often noticeably cheaper.
  * **Compare departure cities**  
If you live in a Tier-2 city, it can be cheaper to fly domestically to **Mumbai or Bengaluru** first and then take the international flight to Bali.


👉 Before booking your trip, you should also read my guide about **[Using Indian Cards in Bali](https://www.baliholidaysecrets.com/using-indian-cards-in-bali/)** , where I explain which cards work, ATMs, Zero-Forex, and cash tips.
* * *
## Best Time to Book Your Flights
For most Indian tourists, the best time to book **flights from India to Bali** depends on two things. India’s holiday calendar and Bali’s peak travel seasons.
If you time it right, you can save a surprising amount on your flight tickets 2026.
  * **The 40 to 45 day rule**  
The cheapest flights from India to Bali usually appear about **6 weeks before departure**. Booking too early or too late often means paying more.
  * **The shoulder season sweet spot**  
Travel in **May, June, September, or October** if you can. The weather in Bali is still great and flights are often cheaper than the August and December rush.
  * **Avoid the wedding season surge**  
November and February are big wedding months in India, and Bali is a popular honeymoon destination. Flights fill up quickly, so it is safer to book at least 3 months in advance.
  * **Look for January to March deals**  
These are often the cheapest months to visit Bali. There can be occasional rain, but you can sometimes grab premium seats for economy-level prices.
  * **Important travel note (Nyepi)**  
Bali’s **Day of Silence** , called [Nyepi](https://www.baliholidaysecrets.com/nyepi/), shuts the entire island down for 24 hours. 


* * *
## Check-in Tips at Indian Airports
![departing mumbai airport to bali](https://www.baliholidaysecrets.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/03/check-in-tips-for-flights-from-india-to-bali.webp) ![](https://www.baliholidaysecrets.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/03/departing-for-flights-from-india-to-bali.webp)
Major airports like **Delhi (T3), Mumbai (T2), and Bengaluru (T2)** can get busy, regardless of time of day and especially during holiday seasons.
A little preparation before your flight from India to Bali can save you a lot of stress at the airport.
Here are a few practical check-in tips most Indians wish they knew earlier.
  * **Arrive 3.5 to 4 hours before departure**  
International flights can have long immigration and security queues, especially for late-night departures. Getting there early gives you enough buffer if the airport is crowded.
  * **Use DigiYatra if your airport supports it**  
If you are flying from Delhi, Mumbai, or Bengaluru, the **DigiYatra app** can speed up airport entry and security checks using facial recognition. Many travellers save 20 to 30 minutes using it.
  * **Complete web check-in early**  
Airlines like IndiGo usually require web check-in between **48 hours and 60 minutes before departure**. It also helps you secure a better seat before the flight fills up. 
  * **Keep your documents ready**  
Carry your passport with at least **6 months validity** and a copy of your **Bali eVOA** if you applied online. Immigration officers often appreciate having a printed copy ready.
  * **Double-check your terminal**  
Some cities have multiple international terminals. A quick check before leaving home can prevent a stressful last-minute terminal transfer.


⚠️ **Note for tourists:** Even if you have a mobile boarding pass, you will still need to visit the **passport and visa verification counter** before checking in your bags.
* * *
## Baggage, Meals, and Extra Fees
Don’t let sneaky “hidden costs” eat into your shopping budget. Budget airlines in particular keep the base fare low, then charge extra for baggage, meals, and seat selection.
Here is the quick breakdown of what is included (and what isn’t) for 2026:
  * **Baggage allowance**
    * **Air India / Singapore Airlines:** Usually **25kg included** , which is ideal if you are travelling with shopping space in mind.
    * **IndiGo:** Often **20kg included** , but some cheaper fares may not include checked baggage at all.
    * **Extra baggage:** Buying at the airport can cost around **₹600 per kg** , so it is cheaper to add weight online before the flight.
  * **In-flight meals**
    * **Full-service airlines (Air India, Singapore Airlines, Thai Airways):** Meals and drinks are normally included.
    * **Budget airlines (IndiGo, AirAsia):** Meals are not included, but you can pre-book one for around **₹400–₹600** , which is cheaper than buying onboard.
  * **Seat selection fees**  
On IndiGo, choosing a window seat or extra legroom can cost around **₹500–₹1,500**. Full-service airlines usually allow standard seat selection for free during web check-in.
  * **Special equipment charges**  
If you are travelling with surfboards, golf clubs, or oversized luggage, airlines may charge a handling fee. This can be around **₹3,000–₹4,000 per sector** , depending on the airline.


* * *
## Arrival Checklist for Indians
![arrival check list - flights from india to bali](https://www.baliholidaysecrets.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/03/arrival-check-list-flights-from-india-to-bali.webp)
Arriving in Bali is the easy part. What usually slows travellers down is paperwork at the airport, especially if you do not have the QR codes ready.
As of 2026, most of the arrival process is digital, so it helps to prepare everything on your phone before you land.
Have these 5 things ready before arrival:
  * **[e-VOA](https://www.baliholidaysecrets.com/visa-bali/) (Electronic Visa on Arrival)**  
Apply through the official immigration website before flying. It costs **IDR 500,000** and lets you use the autogates instead of waiting in the manual visa line.
  * **[Indonesia Arrival Card](https://www.baliholidaysecrets.com/all-indonesia-arrival-card-bali/) (QR code)**  
This is required for everyone. It replaces the old paper form and can be filled in online before you arrive.
  * **[Bali Tourist Tax](https://www.baliholidaysecrets.com/bali-tourist-tax/)**  
All tourists must pay the **IDR 150,000 Bali tourism tax**. It is faster if you pay online before your flight.
  * **[Proof of funds](https://www.baliholidaysecrets.com/proof-of-funds-to-enter-bali/) (for C1 visa travellers)**  
If you applied for the 60-day visa, you may need to show a bank statement with at least **USD 2,000 balance** covering the last few months.
  * **Return ticket and hotel booking**  
Immigration officers do not always ask, but if they do, you need to show proof of onward travel and accommodation.


👉 Read my full guide on [**Bali Visa for Indians** ,](https://www.baliholidaysecrets.com/bali-visa-for-indians/) where I explain visa on arrival, eVOA, required documents, and the latest 2026 entry requirements.
💡 **Tourist tip:** Do not rely on airport Wi-Fi after landing. Take screenshots of all QR codes, visa approvals, and bookings so you can open them instantly at immigration.
* * *
## Pro Tips for Your India to Bali Flight
A few small tricks can make a big difference when taking flights from India to Bali, especially on overnight routes. These are the tips many travellers only learn after doing the trip once.
  * **Choose the left side of the plane**  
When flying into Bali, a window seat on the left side often gives the best views of volcanoes and coastline during descent, especially on clear mornings.
  * **Apply for your visa before you fly**  
Getting the eVOA online lets you use the autogates at Bali Airport. This can save a lot of time compared to standing in the visa queue after landing.
  * **Screenshot all your QR codes**  
Airport Wi-Fi in Bali is not always reliable. Save screenshots of your visa, tourist tax, and arrival card so you can open them instantly at immigration.
  * **Dress for cold cabins and hot weather**  
Long flights from India can feel cold, but Bali will be humid the moment you step outside. Light layers work best.
  * **Pre-book your meal if you fly budget airlines**  
On airlines like IndiGo, vegetarian and special meals can run out. Choosing your meal in advance avoids getting stuck with whatever is left.
  * **Carry a pen in your hand luggage**  
You may still need to fill forms or write details during travel. It sounds old-school, but it saves time.
  * **Bring a power bank in your carry-on**  
Some budget aircraft do not have charging ports. You will need your phone ready for QR codes when you land.
  * **Avoid buying SIM cards at the airport**  
Airport SIM counters are expensive. Buying an eSIM before your trip is usually cheaper and works as soon as you land.
  * **Save your hotel address on your phone**  
Immigration may ask where you are staying, and the arrival card requires the address. Having it ready makes the process quicker.

## FAQs About Indian Flights to Bali
If you are booking flights from India to Bali for the first time, here are the most common things Indian travellers ask before flying. Feel free to drop a comment below if you have more questions.
### How to travel from India to Bali
The easiest way to travel is by air, with several daily flights from India to Bali from cities like Delhi, Mumbai, and Bengaluru. You can choose either a direct flight or a connecting flight via Singapore, Kuala Lumpur, or Bangkok.
### Can you fly direct from India to Bali?
Yes, there are direct flights from India to Bali, mainly from Delhi, Mumbai, and Bengaluru. These are usually the fastest option and take around 7 to 9 hours.
### Are there direct flights from Delhi to Bali?
Yes, Delhi has regular direct flights to Bali, usually operated by Air India or IndiGo. Flight time is normally around 7.5 to 8 hours.
### Are there direct flights from Mumbai to Bali?
Yes, direct flights to Bali also operate from Mumbai, although schedules can vary depending on the season. The flight usually takes around 8 to 8.5 hours.
### Which airline is best to travel to Bali from India?
Air India and IndiGo are the most popular for direct flights to Bali, while Singapore Airlines is often considered the most comfortable option with a short connection. The best choice depends on your budget and how much travel time you want.
### Which city in India has the cheapest flight to Bali?
Bengaluru often has the cheapest flights from India to Bali, followed by Mumbai and Delhi. Prices change often, so it is worth comparing all three before booking.
### How many hours from India to Bali by flight?
Direct flights from India to Bali usually take between 7 and 9 hours. Connecting flights can take anywhere from 10 to 16 hours depending on the layover.
### How long is a flight to Bali from India?
The total time for flights from India to Bali depends on the route. Non-stop flights are under 9 hours, while connecting flights may take half a day.
### What airport do you land at in Bali from India?
All international flights from India to Bali arrive at **Ngurah Rai International Airport (DPS)** , also known as **Denpasar Airport**. This is the main airport for the island and the only one handling international flights.
### What is the best time to book flights from India to Bali?
The best prices for flights from India to Bali are usually found about 40 to 45 days before departure. Booking too late or during peak holiday seasons often means higher prices.
### Which month is cheapest to fly from India to Bali?
January, February, and September are often the cheapest months for flights from India to Bali. These fall outside the main holiday periods in both India and Bali.
### How much do flights from India to Bali cost?
Economy tickets to Bali usually range from ₹32,000 to ₹45,000 in off-peak season. During holidays, prices can go above ₹70,000 depending on demand.
### Do I need return ticket before flying to Bali from India?
Yes, immigration may ask for proof of onward travel when you arrive in Bali. It is safest to have a return ticket or a confirmed flight out of Indonesia before boarding.
### What happens if I miss my flight to Bali because of long security lines in India?
Indian hubs like Delhi and Mumbai are extremely busy for international departures. If you miss your flight, most airlines will charge a heavy “No Show” fee. To avoid this, use the DigiYatra app at the airport and arrive at least **4 hours early** for your Bali flight.

[ Ubud  ](https://www.holidify.com/places/ubud/)
[ Nusa Penida  ](https://www.holidify.com/places/nusa-penida/)

# 297 Places to Visit In Bali
Show on map
[ ![](https://www.holidify.com/places/bali/sightseeing-and-things-to-do.html) ](https://www.holidify.com/places/bali/sightseeing-and-things-to-do.html) x 
### [ 1. Watch Sunset at Tanah Lot  ![Watch Sunset at Tanah Lot](https://www.holidify.com/res/images/patt.png) Top Attraction  **4.8** /5 ](https://www.holidify.com/places/bali/tanah-lot-sightseeing-121464.html)
31 km from city center 
Rocks & Canyons
**Located in : West Bali**
Located on a beautiful offshore rock in Tabanan regency, Bali, Tanah Lot or 'Land on the Sea' is a Hindu shrine believed to be about 500 years old. An amalgamation of Balinese and Hindu mythology, the temple's base is carved with sea snakes and offers a natural source of holy water. Foreigners are not allowed inside the temple but you can walk across to the main temple and watch the sunset. 
Tours starting from   
**₹1,537**
Read More 
### [ 2. Explore Uluwatu  ![Explore Uluwatu](https://www.holidify.com/res/images/patt.png) Great For Family Outing  **4.7** /5 ](https://www.holidify.com/places/bali/ulu-watu-sightseeing-121465.html)
54 km from city center 
Temple
**Located in : Bukit Peninsula**
Situated at the southwestern tip of Bukit in Bali, Uluwatu attracts surfers of all levels from around the world to its exciting shoreline. The waves comprise five world-class surf breaks, and the hotels and restaurants overlooking the Indian ocean provide a magnificent view. The Pura Luru Uluwatu temple is another important attraction in this area. 
Tours starting from   
**₹821**
Read More 
### [ 3. Experience Bali Swing  ![Experience Bali Swing](https://www.holidify.com/res/images/patt.png) Instagram Worthy  **4.5** /5 ](https://www.holidify.com/places/bali/bali-swing-sightseeing-1257938.html)
21 km from city center 
Amusement & Theme Park
**Located in : Ubud**
The Bali Swing is a location with 12 swings and 9 nests with breathtaking views. Some swings are over valleys, some over waterfalls, and some over canyons. It is one of the most famous photography locations in the world. Different tour operators provide packages for Bali Swing. Most popular swings around Bali are Kaja swing, The Ubud swing, Alas Harum Swing, Uma Pakel, and Wana Iri Hidden Hills S (Read More)wing.
Tours starting from   
**₹1,342**
Read More 
### [ 4. Trek to Mount Batur For Sunrise  ![Trek to Mount Batur For Sunrise](https://www.holidify.com/res/images/patt.png) Top Things To Do  **4.7** /5 ](https://www.holidify.com/places/bali/mount-batur-sightseeing-121471.html)
33 km from city center 
Volcano
**Located in : Kintamani**
Called "Gunung Batur" in the local language, Mount Batur is an active volcano in Indonesia. At 1717 meters (around 5,600 feet) above sea level, it offers a spectacular view of its surroundings - the majestic Lake Batur, nestled amongst black lava from the last explosion in 2000, and adjacent to this all, beautiful mountains. 
Tours starting from   
**₹2,277**
Read More

### [ 5. Tirta Empul Temple  ![Tirta Empul Temple](https://www.holidify.com/res/images/patt.png) Popular among Locals  **4.6** /5 ](https://www.holidify.com/places/bali/tirta-empul-sightseeing-121467.html)
26 km from city center 
Temple
**Located in : Ubud**
Built in 962 AD during the reign of Warmadewa dynasty, Pura Tirta Empul or Holy Water Temple, is the legacy of a traditional good versus evil tale. It is home to a holy mountain spring that feeds the Pakerisan river and is revered for its ritual purification by devotees. Tourists are allowed to take the ritual bath. 
Tours starting from   
**₹2,136**
Read More 
### [ 6. Ulun Danu Beratan Temple  ![Ulun Danu Beratan Temple](https://www.holidify.com/res/images/patt.png) **4.7** /5 ](https://www.holidify.com/places/bali/pura-ulun-danu-bratan-sightseeing-121475.html)
11 km from city center 
Temple
**Located in : Kintamani**
Situated west of Lake Bratan in Bedugul, Pura Ulun Danu Bratan is a 17th-century temple dedicated to the Hindu trinity of Brahma-Vishnu-Shiva. Surrounded by the lake which gives it a 'floating' appearance, the temple is constructed in traditional Balinese style with tiered shrines to honour the Hindu gods. The backdrop of the Bedugul mountains makes this a picturesque site of religious worship. 
Tours starting from   
**₹2,135**
Read More 
##  Top Hotels In Bali 
Search Hotels
[ **Best Villas in Bali** 50 options from **₹ 5,117** onwards  ](https://www.holidify.com/hotel-collections/villas-in-bali)
[ **Best Hotels In Bali** 57 options from **₹ 3,420** onwards  ](https://www.holidify.com/hotel-collections/best-hotels-in-bali)

[ View All Hotels In Bali ](https://www.holidify.com/places/bali/hotels-where-to-stay.html)
### [ 7. Day Trip to Gili Trawangan  ![Day Trip to Gili Trawangan](https://www.holidify.com/res/images/patt.png) Day Trip  **4.3** /5 ](https://www.holidify.com/places/gili-trawangan/)
103 km from city center 
Day Trip
A day trip from Bali to Gili Trawangan, part of the Gili Islands, is an adventurous journey. Several fast boat operators offer daily services from Bali, departing from various points like Serangan, Padang Bai, or Sanur to Gili Trawangan, and takes around 1.5 to 2.5 hours. You can engage in snorkeling or diving activities, explore island's beautiful beaches, and visit local markets, cafes and resta (Read More)urants. 
Tours starting from   
**₹1,835**
Read More 
### [ 8. Party At Finns Beach Club  ![Party At Finns Beach Club](https://www.holidify.com/res/images/patt.png) Great For Couples  **4.5** /5 ](https://www.holidify.com/places/bali/finns-beach-club-sightseeing-1265290.html)
13 km from city center 
Bars & Lounges
**Located in : Canggu**
Finns Beach Club is one of Bali's most popular beach clubs, located on Berawa Beach with 170m of oceanfront view. This all-bamboo structure houses nine bars, a sushi bar, and six restaurants. It is a must-visit hangout with four stunning pools and a variety of daybeds and loungers for you to recline on. It is recommended to reserve a table before your arrival. 
Tours starting from   
**₹1,506**
Read More 
### [ 9. Tour Tegalalang Rice Fields  ![Tour Tegalalang Rice Fields](https://www.holidify.com/res/images/patt.png) **4.8** /5 ](https://www.holidify.com/places/bali/tegallalang-rice-terraces-sightseeing-121517.html)
23 km from city center 
Farms & Plantations
**Located in : Ubud**
The Tegallalang Rice Terraces are located about 20 minutes north of Ubud in Bali and are a popular stop for those visiting Kintamani, which falls on the same road. They are famous for the spectacular location where these paddy fields are set in and the two swings where you can click photographs. It is especially popular during sunrise and sunset. 
Tours starting from   
**₹1,921**
Read More 
### [ 10. Trip To Nusa Penida Island  ![Trip To Nusa Penida Island](https://www.holidify.com/res/images/patt.png) Day Trip  ](https://www.holidify.com/places/nusa-penida/)
60 km from city center 
Island
Nusa Penida is the most exotic of the three Nusa Islands and is relatively less traveled. Located in the Klungkung district, it is a little off the shores of Bali. It is popular for activities like snorkeling, scuba diving, and surfing. Known for preserving the endangered bird Bali Starling, famous attractions are the Crystal Bay sand beach and sunset panorama. Boats are available from Bali. 
Tours starting from   
**₹2,557**
Read More 
### [ 11. Scuba Diving  ![Scuba Diving](https://www.holidify.com/res/images/patt.png) Top Things To Do  **4.3** /5 ](https://www.holidify.com/pages/scuba-diving-in-bali-1347.html)
39 km from city center 
Bali's beaches are not just beautiful on the surface, but provide a thrilling experience underwater too. From shipwreck remains at Tulamben to the largest biorock reef site at Pemuteran, Bali has an adventure for all kinds and all levels of divers. It's best to go diving during the dry months, and a great place to start would be the Nusa Penida island. 
Tours starting from   
**₹1,287**
Read More 
### [ 12. Watch Kecak Fire & Dance Show  ![Watch Kecak Fire & Dance Show](https://www.holidify.com/res/images/patt.png) Top Attraction  **4.4** /5 ](https://www.holidify.com/places/bali/kecak-dance-bali-sightseeing-1258307.html)
54 km from city center 
Shows & Theatres
Also known as the Ramayana monkey chant, the Kecak dance is based on the story of Ramayana and is performed in the temples in Bali. The performance includes 150 performers dancing in a circle chanting chak, moving their hands, and wearing checked clothes around their waists. 
Tours starting from   
**₹548**
Read More

Tours starting from   
**₹1,921**
Read More 
### [ 52. Day Trip to Gili Air  ![Day Trip to Gili Air](https://www.holidify.com/res/images/patt.png) Day Trip  ](https://www.holidify.com/places/gili-air-islands/)
108 km from city center 
Day Trip
A day trip from Bali to Gili Air Island is an adventurous excursion to a charming and laid-back paradise. Multiple operators offer daily fast boat services from Bali to Gili Air, departing from various points like Serangan, Padang Bai, or Sanur. The boat journey takes approximately 1.5 to 2.5 hours. Unwind on the island's beautiful beaches, sunbathe, or swim in the crystal-clear waters, enjoying t (Read More)he laid-back coastal ambiance.
Tours starting from   
**₹1,835**
Read More

##  FAQs on Bali 
###  How can I commute within Bali? 
The most common method of transport around Bali is the metered taxi, and these are available in the important parts of Bali such as Denpassar, Ubud and Kuta. In the relatively less urban areas, dokars(horse-carts), ojeks(motorcycles) and bemos(vans) are common means of transport. Dokars are slightly more on the expensive side, while the other two are easily available and cheap as well.  
[Read More](https://www.holidify.com/places/bali/how-to-reach.html#commutingWithin)
###  What are the top activities in Bali? 
The top activities in Bali are Private Ngurah Rai Airport Transfers (DPS) for Bali, [SALE] Bali Private Car Charter, Waterbom Bali Admission Ticket, [SALE] Ayung White Water Rafting, Mount Batur Sunrise Trekking Experience , Bali Safari and Marine Park. 
###  What are the top shopping places in Bali? 
The top shopping places in Bali are Ubud Art Market, Jimbaran Fish Market, Semarapura Market. 
###  What are the top sightseeing places in Bali? 
The top sightseeing places in Bali are Ulu Watu, Mount Batur, Scuba Diving, Tanah Lot, Nusa Penida Island, Goa Gajah. 
###  How much does a package cost for Bali? 
The packages for Bali start at INR 11184 which can be further customized based on number of people, days, hotel category, vehicle type and customizations. You can have a look at all packages [here](https://www.holidify.com/places/bali/packages.html)

# Our Best Indian Recipes for Beginner Cooks
By
[Carl Hanson](https://www.allrecipes.com/author/carl-hanson/)
[Carl Hanson](https://www.allrecipes.com/author/carl-hanson/)
Carl Hanson is a Senior Editor at Allrecipes who has been writing about food and wine for nearly 20 years. He enjoys creating content that informs, entertains, and assists busy home cooks get nourishing meals on the table for their families.
[Allrecipes' editorial guidelines](https://www.allrecipes.com/about-us-6648102#toc-editorial-guidelines)
Published on June 24, 2020
Close
![Indian-Style Chicken and Onions](https://www.allrecipes.com/thmb/4Ya6KMASFBofXvkvg9wbYpwugU8=/1500x0/filters:no_upscale\(\):max_bytes\(150000\):strip_icc\(\):format\(webp\)/3780485_indian-style-chicken-and-onions_buckwheat-queen-5874cff67ef54ae78b7665e3e2dba478.jpg)
Indian-Style Chicken and Onions. Credit: Buckwheat Queen
If you crave Indian food, but don't think you could make it at home, these [Indian recipes](https://www.allrecipes.com/recipes/233/world-cuisine/asian/indian/) are for you. Truth is, Indian food can be surprisingly easy to make. We've gathered a collection of top-rated Indian recipes with simple techniques that aren't time-consuming or complicated. These recipes are perfect for beginning cooks. Mostly what you'll need are some classic Indian spices, which are increasingly easy to find in grocery stores. Once you have those tucked into the spice rack, a whole new world of recipes will open up! 
01 of 18
##  Vegetarian Palak Moong Dal 
[View Recipe](https://www.allrecipes.com/recipe/272433/vegetarian-palak-moong-dal/)
Save
"Indian dal is a classic, soup-like vegetarian comfort food with rich and spicy flavors from cayenne, cumin, and turmeric," says marwamp. "This recipe uses small yellow lentils called moon dal." 
02 of 18
##  Indian Chicken Tikka Masala 
[View Recipe](https://www.allrecipes.com/recipe/262622/indian-chicken-tikka-masala/)
Save
Indian Chicken Tikka Masala. Allrecipes
Ready in about 45 minutes, chicken breasts, garlic, ginger, yogurt, tomatoes, and curry paste come together quickly. "This Indian chicken tikka masala is an easy but flavorful version of everyone's favorite mild-medium curry!" says Keri. "Serve with naan bread and mango chutney. Garnish with additional cilantro leaves." 
03 of 18
##  Indian Chicken Marinade 
[View Recipe](https://www.allrecipes.com/recipe/256359/indian-chicken-marinade/)
Save
This spicy yogurt marinade combines yogurt with soy sauce, honey, garlic, cinnamon, hot pepper sauce, paprika, and ginger. Cover chicken with the marinade and refrigerate for at least 1 hour before grilling. "This spicy yogurt marinade with Indian spices like cinnamon and ginger keeps chicken lovely and moist on the barbeque with a warm spicy flavor," says L Commandeur. 
04 of 18
##  Palak Paneer Curry 
[View Recipe](https://www.allrecipes.com/recipe/267698/palak-paneer-curry/)
Save
Soft cubes of cheese are simmered in a mildly spiced spinach curry. "All of the spinach benefits in an easy-to-make and absolutely delicious Indian curry. Serve with hot naan or roti," says Andreia. 
05 of 18
##  Indian Shrimp Curry 
[View Recipe](https://www.allrecipes.com/recipe/205544/indian-shrimp-curry/)
Save
catherine.drew
This simple shrimp curry is ready in about 30 minutes. "A subtle yet richly flavored curry that complements shrimp perfectly," says Jacqueline B. '"A very authentic taste, and a very easy meal to prepare when you are in a hurry. Make sure you pop the shrimp tails off before adding to skillet. Serve with rice." 
06 of 18
##  Tandoori Chicken II 
[View Recipe](https://www.allrecipes.com/recipe/28011/tandoori-chicken-ii/)
Save
"A paste made from a combination of spices and dried peppers is the secret to this spicy grilled chicken recipe," says DAKOTAGIRL. "No long marinating time is required; the chicken can be prepared in the time it takes to get the coals hot." 
07 of 18
##  Easy Vegetarian Kofta Curry 
[View Recipe](https://www.allrecipes.com/recipe/270805/easy-vegetarian-kofta-curry/)
Save
Quick to make, but full of flavor, this vegetarian curry features meatless vegetable meatballs. "My mother-in-law is Indian and let me in on a secret: The vegetable balls from IKEA® make for a great and ridiculously easy vegetarian kofta curry," says Diana Moutsopoulos. "This is ready in just about 30 minutes, making it perfect for a weeknight. Serve with basmati rice." 
08 of 18
##  Vegan Sweet Potato Chickpea Curry 
[View Recipe](https://www.allrecipes.com/recipe/265472/vegan-sweet-potato-chickpea-curry/)
Save
In this flavorful vegan curry, sweet potatoes, chickpeas, spinach, and coconut milk are seasoned with garam masala, turmeric, and cumin dish. "Yummy vegan curry dish," says Sherri Zeringue D'Argenio. "Serve with basmati rice and naan bread." 
09 of 18
##  Indian Kale with Chickpeas 
[View Recipe](https://www.allrecipes.com/recipe/278215/indian-kale-with-chickpeas/)
Save
Kale is sauteed with chickpeas and Indian spices. "This is a delicious way to cook kale if you are running out of ideas, and it goes great with Indian dishes like dal," says Lady Laff. "Make sure not to overcook the greens!" 
10 of 18
##  Creamy Cashew Chicken Curry 
[View Recipe](https://www.allrecipes.com/recipe/270885/creamy-cashew-chicken-curry/)
Save
Cashew cream makes this curry rich and creamy without dairy. "Cashew cream is remarkably similar to heavy cream in appearance, taste, and in-sauce behavior," says Chef John. "In fact, cashew cream actually thickens up faster. You won't need anything too hard to find, besides maybe garam masala, to make this straightforward recipe. Garnish with whole cashews, lime wedges, and cilantro. Serve with rice, garlic naan, or both!" 
11 of 18
##  One-Pot Vegan Potato-Lentil Curry 
[View Recipe](https://www.allrecipes.com/recipe/267997/one-pot-vegan-potato-lentil-curry/)
Save
This wonderful vegan one-pot curry contains red lentils, potatoes, tomatoes, and onions, flavored with coconut milk, curry paste, and cumin. "This is a great one-pot dish that's tasty and filling," says JessiT. "Careful when measuring out the curry paste -- depending on its spiciness, 1 teaspoon can be enough sometimes. If you are using a mild one, you might need more." 
12 of 18
##  Gobi Manchurian 
[View Recipe](https://www.allrecipes.com/recipe/267609/gobi-manchurian/)
Save
"This spicy Asian dish is one of my favorite gobi (cauliflower) recipes, available at many Indian restaurants," says sunwind. "Occasionally, I even love to bake the cauliflower to lower the calories. Keep in mind, baking will take away from the crispiness of fried cauliflower." 
13 of 18
##  Curry in a Hurry 
[View Recipe](https://www.allrecipes.com/recipe/274063/curry-in-a-hurry/)
Save
This curry combines tomatoes, coconut milk, red lentils, spinach, onion, tomato paste, curry powder, garlic, and cayenne pepper in a multi-functional pressure cooker. It's ready in about 25 minutes. "This red lentil curry in a hurry is made in a multi-functional pressure cooker and is perfect for a quick and hearty vegetarian meal," says Garrett Griffin. 
14 of 18
##  Easy Chicken Madras Recipe 
[View Recipe](https://www.allrecipes.com/recipe/262233/easy-chicken-madras-recipe/)
Save
Caption.
Madras is a region in southern India, which is now modern-day Chennai. It's also a type of curry powder! This chicken recipe combines Madras curry powder with onions, chile peppers, ginger, garlic, chopped tomatoes, and fresh chopped cilantro. Serve with a big spoonful of basmati rice, yogurt, and a fluffy naan. 
15 of 18
##  Red Split Lentils (Masoor Dal) 
[View Recipe](https://www.allrecipes.com/recipe/247204/red-split-lentils-masoor-dal/)
Save
These Indian-inspired red lentils (masoor dal) are flavored with cumin, turmeric, and ginger. "Indian-inspired lentils the whole family will love and babies, too," says katie. "Once the dal and rice are at the desired consistency, it's ready to eat or freeze. Make sure mixture is completely cool before freezing. I use freezer ice trays and small individual glass bowls with lids." 
16 of 18
##  Indian-Style Chicken and Onions 
[View Recipe](https://www.allrecipes.com/recipe/236411/indian-style-chicken-and-onions/)
Save
"Amazing Indian recipe that is simple, tasty, and full of flavor," says phosmane. "This was the first Indian meal I made, and it has stuck around since all of my friends and family love it! Add more or less chiles depending on your spice preference." 
  * [Check out our complete collection of Indian Recipes.](https://www.allrecipes.com/recipes/233/world-cuisine/asian/indian/)


17 of 18
##  Easy Naan 
[View Recipe](https://www.allrecipes.com/recipe/279560/easy-naan/)
Save
Easy Naan | Photo by.
"This naan recipe is deceptively easy!" says Anonymous. "Using your broiler is a great way to make naan. You can choose to make garlic naan or plain naan. Enjoy with your favorite curry!" 
18 of 18
##  More Indian Recipes & Inspiration 
Linda T
