Contemporary Kerala food includes vegetarian and non-vegetarian dishes. Fish and seafood play a major role in Kerala cuisine, as Kerala is a coastal state. An everyday Kerala meal in most households consists of rice with fish curry made of sardines, mackerel, _seer_ fish, king fish, pomfret, prawns, shrimp, sole, anchovy, or parrotfish, (mussels, oysters, crabs, squid, scallops are not rare), and vegetable curry and stir-fried vegetables with or without coconut traditionally known as _[thoran](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Thoran "Thoran")_ or _mizhukkupiratti_. As Kerala has large number of inland water bodies, freshwater fish are also abundant, and part of regular meals. It is common in Kerala to have a breakfast with non-vegetarian dishes in restaurants, in contrast to other states in India. Chicken or mutton stews, lamb, chicken, beef, pork, egg curry, and fish curry with tapioca for breakfast are also widely enjoyed. 
Kerala cuisine reflects its rich trading heritage. Over time, various cuisines have blended with indigenous dishes, while foreign ones have been adapted to local tastes.[[120]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Indian_cuisine#cite_note-120) Significant Arab, Syrian, Portuguese, Dutch, Jewish, and Middle Eastern influences exist in this region's cuisine. 
[![](https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/7/79/Kerala_Style_Prawns_Roast.jpg/250px-Kerala_Style_Prawns_Roast.jpg)](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/File:Kerala_Style_Prawns_Roast.jpg)Kerala style prawns roast–Kerala being a coastal state has different varieties of sea food preparations
[Coconuts](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Coconut "Coconut") grow in abundance in Kerala, so grated coconut and coconut milk are commonly used for thickening and flavouring.[[121]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Indian_cuisine#cite_note-121) Kerala's long coastline and numerous rivers have led to a strong fishing industry in the state, making seafood a common part of the meal. Starchy food like Rice and [tapioca](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Tapioca "Tapioca") forms the major part of Kerala's staple food.[[122]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Indian_cuisine#cite_note-122) Having been a major region of [spice](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Spice "Spice") cultivation and trade for thousands of years, the spices like black pepper, cardamom, clove, ginger, cumin and cinnamon finds extensive use in Kerala cuisine. Kerala _[sadhya](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Sadhya "Sadhya")_ , an elaborate vegetarian banquet prepared for festivals and ceremonies. A full-course _sadhya_ , which consists of rice with about 20 different accompaniments and desserts is the ceremonial meal, eaten usually on celebrations such as marriages, [Onam](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Onam "Onam"), [Vishu](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Vishu "Vishu"), etc. and is served on a [plantain](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Cooking_banana "Cooking banana") leaf. 
Most of Kerala's Hindus, except its [Brahmin](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Brahmin "Brahmin") community, eats fish, chicken, beef, pork, eggs, and mutton.[[123]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Indian_cuisine#cite_note-123) The Brahmin are famed for their vegan cuisine, especially varieties of [_sambar_](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Sambar_\(dish\) "Sambar \(dish\)") and _[rasam](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Rasam_\(dish\) "Rasam \(dish\)")_. A thick vegetable stew popular in South and Central India called _[avial](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Avial "Avial")_ is believed to have originated in southern Kerala. The avial, eaten widely in the state, is an important vegetarian dish in _Kerala sadya_. In most Kerala households, a typical meal consists of rice served along with vegetables and fish or meat dishes. Kerala also has a variety of breakfast dishes like _[idli](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Idli "Idli")_ , _[dosa](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Dosa_\(food\) "Dosa \(food\)")_ , _[appam](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Appam "Appam")_ , _[idiyappam](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Idiyappam "Idiyappam")_ , _[puttu](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Puttu "Puttu")_ , [parotta](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Parotta "Parotta") and _[pathiri](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Pathiri "Pathiri")_ served with sambar, coconut chutney, mutta curry (egg curry), kadala (chickpea) curry, green peas, chicken curry, beef curry and mutton curry.[[124]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Indian_cuisine#cite_note-Laveesh2009\(2\)-124)
The [Muslim](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Muslim "Muslim") community of Kerala blend Arabian, North Indian, and indigenous [Malabari](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Malabar_region "Malabar region") cuisines, using chicken, eggs, beef, and mutton.[[125]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Indian_cuisine#cite_note-125) _Thalassery biryani_ is the only _biryani_ variant, which is of Kerala origin having originated in [Talassery](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Talassery "Talassery"), in [Malabar region](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Malabar_region "Malabar region"). The dish is significantly different from other _biryani_ variants.[[126]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Indian_cuisine#cite_note-126) Snacks like _Pazham nirachathu_ , _Unnakkai_ , _Bread pola_ — made of bread, eggs, milk and a simple masala, Iftar preparations like _Thari kanji_ , _Kozhi pichuporichathu_ (shredded chicken), _Pidi_ — a preparation of rice dumplings dunked in gravy,_Irachi pathiri, Chatti pathiri, Meen pathiri, Neriya pathiri and Kannu vecha pathiri_ – roti varieties usually made of powdered rice, dishes like Kaai curry etc., are also contributions of Muslim community to the broad Kerala cuisine.[[127]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Indian_cuisine#cite_note-127) The [Pathanamthitta](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Pathanamthitta "Pathanamthitta") region is known for _raalan_ and fish curries. _[Appam](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Appam "Appam")_ along with [wine](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Wine "Wine") and curries of duck, pork and cured beef are popular among [Syrian Christians](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Saint_Thomas_Christians "Saint Thomas Christians") in Central Kerala. 
Popular desserts are _[payasam](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Payasam "Payasam")_ (pudding) and _[halwa](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Halwa "Halwa")_. Payasam, especially _Ambalappuzha Paalpayasam_ also known as Gopala Kashayam (Krishnan's potion) prepared at the 17th century [Ambalappuzha Sri Krishna swami temple](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ambalappuzha "Ambalappuzha"), is a delicacy known for its unique and flavourful taste. Interestingly, on each day the paalpayasam is prepared only after (ritualistically) seeking due permission from the presiding deity – [Shri Krishna](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Krishna "Krishna").[[128]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Indian_cuisine#cite_note-128) Kerala has a number of paayasam varieties including but not limited to _Paalpayasam, Vermicelli Payasam, Pradhaman, Ada Pradhaman, Chakka (Jackfruit) Pradhaman, Parippu Paayasam_ and more. Paayasam like Vermicelli Payasam (Semiya payasam) also finds a place in [Iftar](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Iftar "Iftar") feast of Muslim communities in Kerala. 
_Halva_ is one of the most commonly found or easily recognised sweets in bakeries throughout Kerala, and originated from the Gujarathi community in Calicut.[[129]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Indian_cuisine#cite_note-129) Europeans used to call the dish "sweetmeat" due to its texture, and a street in Kozhikode where became named [Sweet Meat Street](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/S.M._Street "S.M. Street") during [colonial rule](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Kerala#Colonial_era "Kerala"). This is mostly made from [_maida_](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Maida_flour "Maida flour") (highly refined wheat), and comes in various flavours, such as banana, _ghee_ or coconut. However, _karutha haluva_ (black _haluva_) made from rice is also very popular. 
### Ladakh
[![](https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/9/99/A_bowl_of_Thukpa.jpg/250px-A_bowl_of_Thukpa.jpg)](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/File:A_bowl_of_Thukpa.jpg)_Thukpa_ is popular in [Ladakh](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ladakh "Ladakh"), [Himachal Pradesh](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Himachal_Pradesh "Himachal Pradesh") and [North-East India](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/North-East_India "North-East India").
Ladakhi cuisine is from the two districts of [Leh](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Leh "Leh") and [Kargil](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Kargil "Kargil") in the union territory of [Ladakh](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ladakh "Ladakh"). Ladakhi food has much in common with [Tibetan food](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Tibetan_food "Tibetan food"), the most prominent foods being _[thukpa](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Thukpa "Thukpa")_ (noodle soup) and _[tsampa](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Tsampa "Tsampa")_ , known in Ladakhi as _ngampe_ (roasted barley flour). Edible without cooking, _tsampa_ makes useful trekking food. 
Strictly Ladakhi dishes include _skyu_ and _chutagi_ , both heavy and rich soup pasta dishes, _skyu_ being made with root vegetables and meat, and _chutagi_ with leafy greens and vegetables.[[130]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Indian_cuisine#cite_note-130) As Ladakh moves toward a cash-based economy, foods from the plains of India are becoming more common.[[131]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Indian_cuisine#cite_note-131)
As in other parts of Central Asia, tea in Ladakh is traditionally made with strong green tea, butter, and salt. It is mixed in a large churn and known as _[gurgur cha](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Butter_tea "Butter tea")_ , after the sound it makes when mixed. Sweet tea (_cha ngarmo_) is common now, made in the Indian style with milk and sugar. Most of the surplus barley that is produced is fermented into _chang_ , an alcoholic beverage drunk especially on festive occasions.[[132]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Indian_cuisine#cite_note-WomensAlliance-132)
### Lakshadweep
The cuisine of [Lakshadweep](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Lakshadweep "Lakshadweep") prominently features seafood and coconut. Local food consists of spicy non-vegetarian and vegetarian dishes. 
The culinary influence of Kerala is quite evident in the cuisines of Lakshadweep, since the island lies in close proximity to Kerala. Coconut and sea fish serve as the foundation of most meals. 
The people of Lakshadweep drink large amounts of [coconut water](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Coconut_water "Coconut water"), which is the most abundant aerated drink on the island. Coconut milk is the base for most of the curries. All the sweet or savoury dishes have a touch of famous [Malabar](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Malabar_Coast "Malabar Coast") spices. Local people also prefer to have _dosa_ , _idlis,_ and various rice dishes.[[133]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Indian_cuisine#cite_note-133)
### Madhya Pradesh
[![](https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/5/53/Baati.jpg/250px-Baati.jpg)](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/File:Baati.jpg)_Daal bafla_ , a popular dish in [Madhya Pradesh](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Madhya_Pradesh "Madhya Pradesh"), [Rajasthan](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Rajasthan "Rajasthan"), and [Gujarat](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Gujarat "Gujarat")
The cuisine in [Madhya Pradesh](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Madhya_Pradesh "Madhya Pradesh") varies regionally. Wheat and meat are common in the north and west of the state, while the wetter south and east are dominated by rice and fish. Milk is a common ingredient in [Gwalior](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Gwalior "Gwalior") and [Indore](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Indore "Indore"). 
The [street food](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Street_food "Street food") of Indore is well known, with shops that have been active for generations.[[134]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Indian_cuisine#cite_note-134) [Bhopal](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Bhopal "Bhopal") is known for meat and fish dishes such as _[rogan josh](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Rogan_josh "Rogan josh")_ , _[korma](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Korma "Korma")_ , _[qeema](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Qeema "Qeema")_ , _[biryani](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Biryani "Biryani")_ , _[pilaf](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Pilaf "Pilaf")_ , and _[kebabs](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Kebab "Kebab")_. On a street named Chatori Gali in old Bhopal, one can find traditional Muslim nonvegetarian fare such as _paya_ soup, _bun kabab_ , and _nalli-nihari_ as some of the specialties.[[135]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Indian_cuisine#cite_note-135)
_[Dal bafla](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Dal_Baati "Dal Baati")_ is a common meal in the region and can be easily found in Indore and other nearby regions, consisting of a steamed and grilled wheat cake dunked in rich _ghee_ , which is eaten with _daal_ and _[ladoos](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Laddu "Laddu")_. 
The culinary specialty of the [Malwa](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Malwa "Malwa") and Indore regions of central Madhya Pradesh is _[poha](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Poha_\(rice\) "Poha \(rice\)")_ (flattened rice); usually eaten at breakfast with _[jalebi](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Jalebi "Jalebi")_.[[136]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Indian_cuisine#cite_note-136)
Beverages in the region include _[lassi](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Lassi "Lassi")_ , [beer](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Beer "Beer"), [rum](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Rum "Rum") and [sugarcane juice](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Sugarcane_juice "Sugarcane juice"). A local liquor is distilled from the flowers of the [mahua tree](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Madhuca_longifolia "Madhuca longifolia"). [Date palm](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Phoenix_dactylifera "Phoenix dactylifera") _[toddy](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Palm_wine "Palm wine")_ is also popular. In tribal regions, a popular drink is the sap of the _sulfi_ tree, which may be alcoholic if it has [fermented](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ethanol_fermentation "Ethanol fermentation"). 
### Maharashtra
Main article: [Maharashtrian cuisine](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Maharashtrian_cuisine "Maharashtrian cuisine")
[![](https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/1/15/Vada_Paav-The_Mumbai_Burger.jpg/250px-Vada_Paav-The_Mumbai_Burger.jpg)](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/File:Vada_Paav-The_Mumbai_Burger.jpg)Vada pav
[Maharashtrian](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Maharashtra "Maharashtra") cuisine is an extensive balance of many different tastes. It includes a range of dishes from mild to very spicy tastes. _[Bajri](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Bajri "Bajri")_ , wheat, rice, _[jowar](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Jowar "Jowar")_ , vegetables, lentils, and fruit form important components of the Maharashtrian diet. 
Popular dishes include _[puran poli](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Puran_poli "Puran poli")_ , _ukdiche[modak](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Modak "Modak")_, _[batata wada](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Batata_wada "Batata wada")_ , _[sabudana khichdi](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Sabudana_Khichadi "Sabudana Khichadi")_ , _masala bhat_ ,[[137]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Indian_cuisine#cite_note-137) _[pav bhaji](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Pav_bhaji "Pav bhaji")_ , and _[wada pav](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Vada_pav "Vada pav")_.[[138]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Indian_cuisine#cite_note-138) _Poha_ or [flattened rice](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Flattened_rice "Flattened rice") is also usually eaten at breakfast. _Kanda poha_[[139]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Indian_cuisine#cite_note-139) and _aloo poha_[[140]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Indian_cuisine#cite_note-140) are some of the dishes cooked for breakfast and snacking in evenings. 
Popular spicy meat dishes include those that originated in the Kolhapur region. These are the Kolhapuri _Sukka_ mutton,[[141]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Indian_cuisine#cite_note-141) _pandhra rassa_ ,[[142]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Indian_cuisine#cite_note-142) and _tabmda rassa_.[[143]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Indian_cuisine#cite_note-143) _[Shrikhand](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Shrikhand "Shrikhand")_ , a sweet dish made from [strained yogurt](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Strained_yogurt "Strained yogurt"), is a main dessert of Maharashtrian cuisine.[[144]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Indian_cuisine#cite_note-144)
[![](https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/0/03/Vedhmi.jpg/250px-Vedhmi.jpg)](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/File:Vedhmi.jpg)[Puran poli](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Puran_poli "Puran poli")
The cuisine of Maharashtra can be divided into two major sections, the coastal and the interior. The [Konkan](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Konkan "Konkan"), on the coast of the [Arabian Sea](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Arabian_Sea "Arabian Sea"), has its own type of cuisine, a homogeneous combination of [Malvani](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Malvan "Malvan"), [Goud Saraswat Brahmin](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Goud_Saraswat_Brahmin "Goud Saraswat Brahmin"), and [Goan cuisine](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Goan_cuisine "Goan cuisine"). In the interior of Maharashtra, the [Paschim Maharashtra](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Paschim_Maharashtra "Paschim Maharashtra"), [Khandesh](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Khandesh "Khandesh"), [Vidarbha](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Vidarbha "Vidarbha") and [Marathwada](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Marathwada "Marathwada") areas have their own distinct cuisines. 
The cuisine of Vidarbha uses [groundnuts](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Peanut "Peanut"), poppy seeds, [jaggery](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Jaggery "Jaggery"), wheat, _[jowar](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Sorghum_bicolor "Sorghum bicolor")_ , and _[bajra](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Pearl_millet#Culinary_use "Pearl millet")_ extensively. A typical meal consists of rice, _[roti](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Roti "Roti")_ , _[poli](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Puran_poli "Puran poli")_ , or _[bhakar](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Jhunka "Jhunka")_ , along with _[varan](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Varan_bhaat "Varan bhaat")_ and _aamtee_[[145]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Indian_cuisine#cite_note-145)—lentils and spiced vegetables. Cooking is common with different types of oil. 
[Savji](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Savji "Savji") food from Vidarbha is well known all over Maharashtra. Savji dishes are very spicy and oily. Savji mutton curries are very famous. 
Like other coastal states, an enormous variety of vegetables, fish, and coconuts exists, where they are common ingredients. Peanuts and [cashews](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Cashew "Cashew") are often served with vegetables. Grated coconuts are used to flavour many types of dishes, but coconut oil is not widely used; peanut oil is preferred.[[146]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Indian_cuisine#cite_note-Corporation2007-146)
_[Kokum](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Kokum "Kokum")_ , most commonly served chilled, in an appetiser-digestive called _[sol kadhi](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Solkadhi "Solkadhi")_ , is prevalent. During summer, Maharashtrians consume _[panha](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Aam_panna "Aam panna")_ , a drink made from raw mango.[[147]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Indian_cuisine#cite_note-147)[[148]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Indian_cuisine#cite_note-148)
#### Malwani

[![](https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/9/92/Pathrado_%28Steamed_Taro_leaves%29.jpg/250px-Pathrado_%28Steamed_Taro_leaves%29.jpg)](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/File:Pathrado_\(Steamed_Taro_leaves\).jpg)Pathrado (Steamed Taro leaves)
Malwani cuisine is a specialty of the tropical area which spans from the shore of [Deogad Malwan](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Devgad_taluka "Devgad taluka") to the southern Maharashtrian border with [Goa](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Goa "Goa"). The unique taste and flavour of Malwani cuisine comes from Malwani _masala_ and use of coconut and [_kokam_](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Garcinia_indica "Garcinia indica"). 
The staple foods are rice and fish. Various kinds of red and green fish, prawns, crab, and shellfish curries (also called _mashacha sar_ in the Malwani language) are well known, along with _kombadi_ (chicken) _wade_ and mutton prepared Malwani style. _Mohari_ mutton is also one of the distinct delicacies of Malwani cuisine. 
A large variety of fish is available in the region, which include _[surmai](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Indo-Pacific_king_mackerel "Indo-Pacific king mackerel")_ , _[karali](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Dorab_wolf-herring "Dorab wolf-herring")_ , _[bangada](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Indian_mackerel "Indian mackerel")_ , _bombil_ ([Bombay duck](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Bombay_duck "Bombay duck")), _paplet_ ([pomfret](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Pomfret "Pomfret")), _halwa_ , _tarali_ , _suandale_ , _kolambi_ (prawns), _tisari_ ([shellfish](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Shellfish "Shellfish")), _kalwa_ ([stone fish](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Stone_fish "Stone fish")) and _kurli_ (crab). 
All these fish are available in dried form, including prawns, which are known as _sode_. Local curries and _chatanis_ are also prepared with dried fish. 
Different types of rice breads and pancakes add to the variety of Malwani cuisine and include _tandlachi bhakari_ ,[[149]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Indian_cuisine#cite_note-149) _ghawane_ , _amboli_ ,[[150]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Indian_cuisine#cite_note-150) _[patole](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Patoleo "Patoleo")_ , _[appe](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Appam "Appam")_ , _tandalachi_ and _shavai_ (rice noodles). These rice breads can be eaten specially flavoured with coconut milk, fish curries, and chicken or mutton curries. 
_Sole kadi_ made from _kokam_ and coconut milk is a signature appetiser drink. For vegetarians, Malwani delicacies include _alloochi bhaji_ , _alloochi gathaya_ , _kalaya watanyacha_ , and _[sambara](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Sambar_\(dish\) "Sambar \(dish\)")_ (black gram stew). 
The sweets and desserts include _ukadiche modak_ ,[[151]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Indian_cuisine#cite_note-151) _Malawani khaje_ , _khadakahde kundiche ladu_ , _shegdanyache ladu_ , _tandalchi kheer_ , and _tandalachi shavai ani ras_ (specially flavoured with coconut milk). 
### Manipur
Main article: [Manipuri cuisine](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Manipuri_cuisine "Manipuri cuisine")
[![](https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/8/83/Soibum_yendem_eromba.jpg/250px-Soibum_yendem_eromba.jpg)](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/File:Soibum_yendem_eromba.jpg)_Eromba_ , here vegetarian, is a popular Manipuri dish.
[Manipuri](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Manipur "Manipur") cuisine is represented by the cuisine of the [Meitei people](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Meitei_people "Meitei people") who form the majority population in the central plain. Meitei food are simple, tasty, organic and healthy. Rice with local seasonal vegetables and fish form the main diet. 
Most of the dishes are cooked like vegetable stew, flavoured with either [fermented fish](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Fermented_fish "Fermented fish") called _ngari_ , or dried and smoked fish. 
The most popular Manipuri dish is _[eromba](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Eromba "Eromba")_ , a preparation of boiled and mashed vegetables, often including carrots, [potatoes](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Potato "Potato") or [beans](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Bean "Bean"), mixed with [chilli](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Chili_pepper "Chili pepper") and roasted fermented [fish](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Fish_as_food "Fish as food"). 
Another popular dish is the savoury cake called _paknam_ , made of a lentil flour stuffed with various ingredients such as banana inflorescence, [mushrooms](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Edible_mushroom "Edible mushroom"), fish, vegetables etc., and baked covered in [turmeric](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Turmeric "Turmeric") leaves. 
Along with spicy dishes, a mild side dish of steamed or boiled sweet vegetables are often served in the daily meals. The Manipuri salad dish called _[singju](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Singju "Singju")_ , made of finely julienned [cabbage](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Cabbage "Cabbage"), green [papaya](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Papaya "Papaya"), and other vegetables, and garnished with local herbs, toasted [sesame](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Sesame "Sesame") powder and lentil flour is extremely popular locally, and often found sold in small street side vendors. 
_Singju_ is often served with _bora_ , which are fritters of various kinds, and also _kanghou_ , or oil-fried spicy veggies.[[152]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Indian_cuisine#cite_note-152) Cooked and fermented [soybean](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Soybean "Soybean") is a popular condiment in all Manipuri kitchens. 
The staple diet of Manipur consists of rice, fish, large varieties of leafy vegetables (of both aquatic and terrestrial). [Manipuris](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Manipur "Manipur") typically raise vegetables in a [kitchen](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Kitchen "Kitchen") garden and rear fishes in small ponds around their house. Since the vegetables are either grown at home or obtained from local market, the cuisines are very seasonal, each season having its own special vegetables and preparations. 
The taste is very different from mainland Indian cuisines because of the use of various aromatic [herbs](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Herbs "Herbs") and [roots](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Root "Root") that are peculiar to the region. They are however very similar to the cuisines of [Southeast](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Southeast_Asia "Southeast Asia"), [East](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/East_Asia "East Asia"), and [Central Asia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Central_Asia "Central Asia"), [Siberia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Siberia "Siberia"), [Micronesia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Micronesia "Micronesia") and [Polynesia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Polynesia "Polynesia"). 
### Meghalaya
Main article: [Meghalayan cuisine](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Meghalayan_cuisine "Meghalayan cuisine")
[![](https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/4/45/Jadoh_%28Meghalaya.JPG/250px-Jadoh_%28Meghalaya.JPG)](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/File:Jadoh_\(Meghalaya.JPG)_Jadoh_ with mutton is characteristic of [Meghalayan](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Meghalaya "Meghalaya") cuisine.
[Meghalayan](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Meghalaya "Meghalaya") cuisine is unique and different from other Northeastern Indian states.[[153]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Indian_cuisine#cite_note-Lonely_Planet_blue_list:_the_best_in_travel_2007-153) Spiced meat is common, from goats, pigs, fowl, ducks, chickens, and cows. In the [Khasi](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Khasi_Hills "Khasi Hills") and [Jaintia Hills districts](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Jaintia_Hills_district "Jaintia Hills district"), common foods include _jadoh_ , _ki kpu_ , _[tung-rymbai](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Tung-rymbai "Tung-rymbai")_ ,[[154]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Indian_cuisine#cite_note-154) and pickled [bamboo shoots](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Bamboo_shoot "Bamboo shoot"). 
Other common foods in Meghalaya include _minil songa_ (steamed [sticky rice](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Glutinous_rice "Glutinous rice")) and _sakkin gata_. 
Like other tribes in the northeast, the Garos ferment [rice beer](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Rice_wine "Rice wine"), which they consume in religious rites and secular celebrations.[[155]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Indian_cuisine#cite_note-Bareh2001-155)
### Mizoram
Main article: [Mizo cuisine](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Mizo_cuisine "Mizo cuisine")
The cuisine of [Mizoram](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Mizoram "Mizoram") differs from that of most of India, though it shares characteristics to other regions of [Northeast India](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Northeast_India "Northeast India") and North India. 
Rice is the staple food of Mizoram, while Mizos love to add non-vegetarian ingredients in every dish. Fish, chicken, pork and beef are popular meats among Mizos. Dishes are served on fresh [banana leaves](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Banana_leaf#In_Indian_cuisine "Banana leaf"). Most of the dishes are cooked in [mustard oil](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Mustard_oil#Pressed_oil "Mustard oil"). 
Meals tend to be less spicy than in most of India. Mizos love eating boiled vegetables along with rice. A popular dish is _bai_ , made from boiling vegetables (spinach, eggplant, beans, and other leafy vegetables) with _bekang_ (fermented soya beans) or _sa-um_ , fermented pork fat served with rice. _Sawhchiar_ is another common dish, made of rice and cooked with pork or chicken.[[156]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Indian_cuisine#cite_note-156)[[157]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Indian_cuisine#cite_note-157)
### Nagaland
Main article: [Naga cuisine](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Naga_cuisine "Naga cuisine")
[![](https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/e/e7/Naga_style_smoked_Pork_ribs_with_bamboo_shoots_and_rice.jpg/250px-Naga_style_smoked_Pork_ribs_with_bamboo_shoots_and_rice.jpg)](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/File:Naga_style_smoked_Pork_ribs_with_bamboo_shoots_and_rice.jpg)Naga style smoked Pork ribs with bamboo shoots and rice
The cuisine of [Nagaland](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Nagaland "Nagaland") reflects that of the [Naga people](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Naga_people "Naga people"). It is known for exotic pork meats cooked with simple and flavourful ingredients,[[158]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Indian_cuisine#cite_note-Laveesh2009\(3\)-158) like the extremely hot _[bhut jolokia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Bhut_jolokia "Bhut jolokia")_ (ghost chilli) pepper, fermented bamboo shoots, and _[akhuni](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Akhuni "Akhuni")_ (fermented [soya beans](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Soybean "Soybean")). Another unique and strong ingredient used by the Naga people, is the fermented fish known as _ngari_. 
Fresh herbs and other local greens also feature prominently in the Naga cuisine. The Naga use oil sparingly, preferring to ferment, dry, and smoke their meats and fish. 
Traditional homes in Nagaland have external kitchens that serve as [smokehouses](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Smokehouse "Smokehouse").[[159]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Indian_cuisine#cite_note-Chopra1992-159)
A typical meal consists of rice, meat, a chutney, a couple of stewed or steamed vegetable dishes, flavoured with _ngari_ or _akhuni_. Desserts usually consist of fresh fruits. 
### Odisha
Main article: [Cuisine of Odisha](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Cuisine_of_Odisha "Cuisine of Odisha")
The cuisine of [Odisha](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Odisha "Odisha") relies heavily on local ingredients. Flavours are usually subtle and delicately spiced. Fish and other seafood, such as crab and shrimp, are very popular, and chicken and mutton are also consumed. 
[![](https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/e/e7/RJ..._1.jpg/250px-RJ..._1.jpg)](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/File:RJ..._1.jpg)Pakhala Platter
_[Pakhala](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Pakhala "Pakhala")_ , a dish made of rice, water, and [_dahi_](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Dahi_\(curd\) "Dahi \(curd\)") (yogurt), that is fermented overnight, is very popular in summer in rural areas.[[160]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Indian_cuisine#cite_note-TharuLalita1993-160) [Odias](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Odia_people "Odia people") are very fond of sweets, so [dessert](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Dessert "Dessert") follows most meals. 
_[Panch phutana](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Panch_phutana "Panch phutana")_ , a mix of cumin, mustard, fennel, fenugreek and _kalonji_ (_[nigella](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Nigella_sativa#Culinary_uses "Nigella sativa")_), is widely used for flavouring vegetables and _dals_ ,[[161]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Indian_cuisine#cite_note-161) while _[garam masala](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Garam_masala "Garam masala")_ and turmeric are commonly used for meat-based curries. 
Popular Odia dishes include _[arna](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Cooked_rice "Cooked rice")_ , _[kanika](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Kanika_\(food\) "Kanika \(food\)")_ , _[dalma](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Cuisine_of_Odisha#Dal "Cuisine of Odisha")_ , _[khata](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ouu_khatta "Ouu khatta")_ (_tamato_ and _oou_), _dali_ (different types of lentils, i.e. _harada_ [[red gram](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Pigeon_pea "Pigeon pea")], _muga_ [[green gram](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Mung_bean "Mung bean")], _kolatha_ [[horsegram](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Horsegram "Horsegram")], etc.), spinach and other green leaves, and _alu-bharta_ (mashed potato)[[162]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Indian_cuisine#cite_note-162) along with _pakhala_. 
Odisha and neighbouring [West Bengal](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/West_Bengal "West Bengal") both claim to be the origin of _[rasgulla](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Rasgulla "Rasgulla")_ , each state having a [geographical indication](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Geographical_indication "Geographical indication") for their regional variety of the dessert.[[59]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Indian_cuisine#cite_note-rasgulla_1-59)[[60]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Indian_cuisine#cite_note-rasgulla_2-60) Odisha is also known for its _[chhena](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Chhena "Chhena")_ - based sweets, including _[chhena poda](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Chhena_poda "Chhena poda")_ , _[chhena gaja](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Chhena_gaja "Chhena gaja")_ , _[chhena jhili](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Chhena_jhili "Chhena jhili")_ , and _[rasabali](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Rasabali "Rasabali")_. 
### Puducherry
The union territory of [Puducherry](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Puducherry_\(union_territory\) "Puducherry \(union territory\)") was a [French colony](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/French_India "French India") for around 200 years, making [French cuisine](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/French_cuisine "French cuisine") a strong influence on the area. [Tamil cuisine](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Tamil_cuisine "Tamil cuisine") is eaten by the territory's [Tamil](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Tamil_people "Tamil people") majority. The influence of the neighbouring areas, such as [Andhra Pradesh](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Andhra_Pradesh "Andhra Pradesh") and Kerala, is also visible on the territory's cuisine. 
Some favourite dishes include coconut curry, _[tandoori](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Tandoor "Tandoor")_ potato, _[soya dosa](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Dosa_\(food\) "Dosa \(food\)")_ , _podanlangkai_ ([snake gourd](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Snake_gourd "Snake gourd") [chutney](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Chutney "Chutney")),[[163]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Indian_cuisine#cite_note-163) curried vegetables, [stuffed cabbage](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Cabbage_roll "Cabbage roll"), and [baked beans](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Baked_beans "Baked beans").[[164]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Indian_cuisine#cite_note-164)
### Punjab
Main article: [Punjabi cuisine](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Punjabi_cuisine "Punjabi cuisine")
[![](https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/e/e1/Chickentandoori.jpg/250px-Chickentandoori.jpg)](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/File:Chickentandoori.jpg)[_Tandoori_ chicken](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Tandoori_chicken "Tandoori chicken") is a popular grilled dish in Punjab.
The cuisine of [Punjab](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Punjab,_India "Punjab, India") is known for its diverse range of dishes. It is closely related to the cuisine of the neighbouring Punjab province of Pakistan. 
The state, being an agriculture centre, is abundant with whole grains, vegetables, and fruits. Home-cooked and restaurant Punjabi cuisine can vary significantly. 
Restaurant-style Punjabi cooking puts emphasis on creamy textured foods by using _ghee_ , butter and cream, while home-cooked meals centre around whole wheat, rice, and other ingredients flavoured with various kinds of _[masalas](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Masala_\(spice\) "Masala \(spice\)")_.[[165]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Indian_cuisine#cite_note-Dalal2007-165)
Common dishes cooked at home are _[roti](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Roti "Roti")_ with _[daal](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Dal "Dal")_ and _[dahi](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Dahi_\(curd\) "Dahi \(curd\)")_ (yogurt) with a side [chutney](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Chutney "Chutney") and [salad](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Salad "Salad") that includes raw onion, tomato, cucumber, etc. 
The meals are also abundant of local and seasonal vegetables usually sautéed with spices such as cumin, dried coriander, red chilli powder, turmeric, black cloves, etc. [Masala chai](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Masala_chai "Masala chai") is a favourite drink and is consumed in everyday life and at special occasions. 
Many regional differences exist in the Punjabi cuisine based on traditional variations in cooking similar dishes, food combinations, preference of spice combination, etc. It is clear that "the food is simple, robust, and closely linked to the land."[[166]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Indian_cuisine#cite_note-Sen2004-166)
Certain dishes exclusive to Punjab, such as _[makki di roti](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Makki_di_roti "Makki di roti")_ and _[sarson da saag](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Sarson_da_saag "Sarson da saag")_ ,[[167]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Indian_cuisine#cite_note-Dalal2007\(3\)-167) _[dal makhani](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Dal_makhani "Dal makhani")_ , and others are a favourite of many. 
The _masala_ in a Punjabi dish traditionally consists of onion, garlic, ginger, cumin, _[garam masala](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Garam_masala "Garam masala")_ , salt, turmeric, tomatoes sauteed in mustard oil. _[Tandoori](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Tandoori "Tandoori")_ food is a Punjabi specialty. Dishes like [Bhatti da murgh](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Bhatti_da_murgh&action=edit&redlink=1 "Bhatti da murgh \(page does not exist\)") also known as _tandoori chicken_ , [Chicken hariyali kabab](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Chicken_hariyali_kabab&action=edit&redlink=1 "Chicken hariyali kabab \(page does not exist\)"), [Achari paneer tikka](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Achari_paneer_tikka&action=edit&redlink=1 "Achari paneer tikka \(page does not exist\)"), [fish ajwaini tikka](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Fish_ajwaini_tikka&action=edit&redlink=1 "Fish ajwaini tikka \(page does not exist\)") and [Amritsari kulcha](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Amritsari_kulcha "Amritsari kulcha") are some popular tandoori foods from Punjab. 
[![](https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/b/ba/New_Punjab_Club_food_spread.jpg/250px-New_Punjab_Club_food_spread.jpg)](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/File:New_Punjab_Club_food_spread.jpg)Punjabi platter.
Common meat dishes in this region are Bhakra curry (goat) and fish dishes.[[168]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Indian_cuisine#cite_note-Ng1995-168) Dairy products are regularly enjoyed and usually accompany main meals in the form of [_dahi_](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Dahi_\(curd\) "Dahi \(curd\)"), milk, and milk-derived products such as _[lassi](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Lassi "Lassi")_ , _[paneer](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Paneer "Paneer")_ , and more. 
Punjab has a large number of people following the [Sikh](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Sikh "Sikh") religion who traditionally follow a vegetarian diet (which includes plant-derived foods, milk, and milk by-products. See [diet in Sikhism](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Diet_in_Sikhism "Diet in Sikhism")) in accordance to their beliefs. 
No description of Punjabi cuisine is complete without the myriad of famous desserts, such as _[kheer](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Kheer "Kheer")_ , _[gajar ka halwa](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Gajar_ka_halwa "Gajar ka halwa")_ , _sooji_ (cream of wheat) _[halwa](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Halwa "Halwa")_ , _[rasmalai](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Rasmalai "Rasmalai")_ , _[gulab jamun](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Gulab_jamun "Gulab jamun")_ and _[jalebi](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Jalebi "Jalebi")_. Most desserts are _ghee_ or dairy-based, use nuts such as almonds, walnuts, pistachios, cashews, and, raisins. 
Many of the most popular elements of [Anglo-Indian cuisine](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Anglo-Indian_cuisine "Anglo-Indian cuisine"), such as _tandoori_ foods, _[naan](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Naan "Naan")_ , _[pakoras](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Pakora "Pakora")_ and vegetable dishes with _[paneer](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Paneer "Paneer")_ , are derived from Punjabi styles.[[169]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Indian_cuisine#cite_note-169)
Punjabi food is well liked in the world for its flavours, spices, and, versatile use of produce, and so it is one of the most popular cuisines from the sub-continent. Last but not least are the [_chhole bhature_](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Chole_bhature "Chole bhature") and _chhole kulche_[[170]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Indian_cuisine#cite_note-170) which are famous all over the North India. 
### Rajasthan
Main article: [Rajasthani cuisine](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Rajasthani_cuisine "Rajasthani cuisine")
[![](https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/5/51/The_delicious_Rajasthani_food.png/250px-The_delicious_Rajasthani_food.png)](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/File:The_delicious_Rajasthani_food.png)Rajasthani _thali_
Cooking in [Rajasthan](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Rajasthan "Rajasthan"), an arid region, has been strongly shaped by the availability of ingredients. Food is generally cooked in milk or _ghee_ , making it quite rich. [Gram flour](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Gram_flour "Gram flour") is a mainstay of [Marwari](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Marwar "Marwar") food mainly due to the scarcity of vegetables in the area.[[171]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Indian_cuisine#cite_note-Wright2005-171)
Historically, food that could last for several days and be eaten without heating was preferred. Major dishes of a Rajasthani meal may include _[daal-baati](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Baati "Baati")_ , _tarfini_ , _raabdi_ , _[ghevar](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ghevar "Ghevar")_ , _bail-gatte_ , _panchkoota_ , _chaavadi_ , _[laapsi](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Laapsi "Laapsi")_ , _[kadhi](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Kadhi "Kadhi")_ and _[boondi](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Boondi "Boondi")_. Typical [snacks](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Snack_food "Snack food") include _[bikaneri bhujia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Bikaneri_bhujia "Bikaneri bhujia")_ , _[mirchi bada](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Mirchi_bada "Mirchi bada")_ , _[pyaaj kachori](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Pyaaj_Kachori "Pyaaj Kachori")_ , and _[dal kachori](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Kachori "Kachori")_. 
_[Daal-baati](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Baati "Baati")_ is the most popular dish prepared in the state. It is usually supplemented with _[choorma](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Churma "Churma")_ , a mixture of finely ground baked _rotis_ , sugar and _ghee_.[[172]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Indian_cuisine#cite_note-Laveesh2009\(4\)-172)
[Rajasthan](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Rajasthan "Rajasthan") is also influenced by the [Rajput](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Rajput "Rajput") community who have liking for meat dishes. Their diet consisted of [game meat](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Game_meat "Game meat") and gave birth to dishes like _[laal maans](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Laal_maans "Laal maans")_ , _safed maas_ ,[[173]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Indian_cuisine#cite_note-173) _khad khargosh_[[174]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Indian_cuisine#cite_note-174) and _jungli maas_.[[175]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Indian_cuisine#cite_note-175)
### Sikkim
Main article: [Sikkimese cuisine](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Sikkimese_cuisine "Sikkimese cuisine")
[![](https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/5/53/Dal_bhat_Birethani.jpg/250px-Dal_bhat_Birethani.jpg)](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/File:Dal_bhat_Birethani.jpg)Nepalese-style _dal bhat_ is popular in Sikkim.
In [Sikkim](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Sikkim "Sikkim"), various ethnic groups such as the [Nepalese](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Nepalese_people "Nepalese people"), [Bhutias](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Bhutias "Bhutias"), and [Lepchas](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Lepcha_people "Lepcha people") have their own distinct cuisines. [Nepalese cuisine](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Nepalese_cuisine "Nepalese cuisine") is very popular in this area. 
Rice is the staple food of the area, and meat and dairy products are also widely consumed. For centuries, traditional fermented foods and beverages have constituted about 20 percent of the local diet. 
Depending on altitudinal variation, finger millet, wheat, buckwheat, barley, vegetables, potatoes, and soybeans are grown. _[Dhindo](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Dhindo "Dhindo")_ , _[daal bhat](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Dal_bhat "Dal bhat")_ , _[gundruk](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Gundruk "Gundruk")_ , _[momo](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Momo_\(dumpling\) "Momo \(dumpling\)")_ , _gya thuk_ , _[ningro](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Fiddlehead#Indian_cuisine "Fiddlehead")_ , _phagshapa_ , and _[sel roti](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Sel_roti "Sel roti")_ are some of the local dishes. 
Alcoholic drinks are consumed by both men and women. Beef is eaten by [Bhutias](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Bhutias "Bhutias").[[176]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Indian_cuisine#cite_note-GallHobby2009-176)
### Sindh
Main article: [Sindhi cuisine](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Sindhi_cuisine "Sindhi cuisine")
See also: [Sindhis in India](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Sindhis_in_India "Sindhis in India")
[![](https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/c/c6/Sindhi_Sai_Bhaji.JPG/250px-Sindhi_Sai_Bhaji.JPG)](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/File:Sindhi_Sai_Bhaji.JPG)_Sai bhaji_ is a Sindhi dish.
Sindhi cuisine refers to the native cuisine of the [Sindhi people](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Sindhi_people "Sindhi people") from the [Sindh](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Sindh "Sindh") region, now in [Pakistan](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Pakistan "Pakistan"). While Sindh is not geographically a part of modern India, its culinary traditions persist,[[177]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Indian_cuisine#cite_note-177) due to the sizeable number of Hindu Sindhis who migrated to India following the [independence](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Partition_of_India "Partition of India") of [Pakistan](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Pakistan "Pakistan") in 1947, especially in Sindhi enclaves such as [Ulhasnagar](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ulhasnagar "Ulhasnagar") and [Gandhidam](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Gandhidam "Gandhidam"). 
A typical meal in many Sindhi households includes wheat-based flatbread (_[phulka](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Phulka "Phulka")_) and rice accompanied by two dishes, one with gravy and one dry. [Lotus stem](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Lotus_stem "Lotus stem") (known as _kamal kakri_) is also used in Sindhi dishes. Cooking vegetables by deep frying is common. 
Some regular Sindhi dishes are _sindhi kadhi_ ,[[178]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Indian_cuisine#cite_note-178) _[sai bhaji](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Sai_bhaji "Sai bhaji")_ , _koki_[[179]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Indian_cuisine#cite_note-179) and _besan bhaji_. Ingredients frequently used are mango powder, tamarind, [_kokum_ flowers](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Garcinia_indica#Culinary_uses "Garcinia indica"), and dried pomegranate seeds.[[180]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Indian_cuisine#cite_note-MoorjaniSnortum2005-180)
### Tamil Nadu
Main article: [Cuisine of Tamil Nadu](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Cuisine_of_Tamil_Nadu "Cuisine of Tamil Nadu")
[![](https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/5/5d/Veg_Full_Meals_in_Tamil_Nadu.JPG/250px-Veg_Full_Meals_in_Tamil_Nadu.JPG)](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/File:Veg_Full_Meals_in_Tamil_Nadu.JPG)Vegetarian meals in Tamil Nadu are traditionally served on a plantain leaf. [![](https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/1/1b/A_banana_leaf_with_Tamil_bites_-_Murukku.jpg/250px-A_banana_leaf_with_Tamil_bites_-_Murukku.jpg)](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/File:A_banana_leaf_with_Tamil_bites_-_Murukku.jpg)[Murukku](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Murukku "Murukku")
[Tamil Nadu](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Tamil_Nadu "Tamil Nadu") is noted for its deep belief that serving food to others is a service to humanity, as is common in many regions of India. The region has a rich cuisine involving both traditional non-vegetarian and vegetarian dishes. 
[Tamil](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Tamil_Nadu "Tamil Nadu") food is characterised by its use of rice, legumes, and lentils, along with distinct aromas and flavours achieved by the blending of spices such as [mustard](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Mustard_\(condiment\) "Mustard \(condiment\)"), [curry leaves](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Curry_tree "Curry tree"), [tamarind](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Tamarind "Tamarind"), [coriander](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Coriander "Coriander"), [ginger](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ginger "Ginger"), [garlic](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Garlic "Garlic"), [chilli pepper](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Chili_pepper "Chili pepper"), [cinnamon](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Cinnamon "Cinnamon"), [clove](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Clove "Clove"), [cardamom](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Cardamom "Cardamom"), [cumin](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Cumin "Cumin"), [nutmeg](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Nutmeg "Nutmeg"), [coconut](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Coconut "Coconut") and [rose water](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Rose_water "Rose water"). 
The traditional way of eating involves being seated on the floor, having the food served on a plantain leaf, and using the right hand to eat. After the meal the plantain leaf is discarded but becomes food for free-ranging cattle and goats. 
A meal (called _saapadu_) consists of rice with other typical Tamil dishes on a plantain leaf. A typical Tamilian would eat on a plantain leaf as it is believed to give a different flavour and taste to food. Also growing in popularity are stainless-steel trays, plates with a selection of different dishes in small bowls. 
Tamil food is characterised by _[tiffin](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Tiffin "Tiffin")_ , which is a light food taken for breakfast or dinner, and meals which are usually taken during lunch. The word "curry" is derived from the [Tamil](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Tamil_language "Tamil language") _kari_ , meaning something similar to "sauce".[[181]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Indian_cuisine#cite_note-Heldke2003-181)[[182]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Indian_cuisine#cite_note-Cummings2000-182)
Southern regions such as [Tirunelveli](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Tirunelveli "Tirunelveli"), [Madurai](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Madurai "Madurai"), [Paramakudi](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Paramakudi "Paramakudi"), [Karaikudi](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Karaikudi "Karaikudi"), [Chettinad](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Chettinad "Chettinad") and [Kongu Nadu](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Kongu_Nadu "Kongu Nadu") are noted for their spicy non-vegetarian dishes.[[183]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Indian_cuisine#cite_note-Kapoor-183)[[184]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Indian_cuisine#cite_note-FodorCurtis1964-184) _[Dosa](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Dosa_\(food\) "Dosa \(food\)")_ , _[idli](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Idli "Idli")_ , _[pongal](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Pongal_\(dish\) "Pongal \(dish\)")_ and _[biryani](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Biryani "Biryani")_ are some of the popular dishes that are eaten with _[chutney](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Chutney "Chutney")_ and _sambar_. Fish and other seafoods are also very popular, because the state is located on the coast. Chicken and goat meat are the predominantly consumed meats in Tamil Nadu. 
A typical Tamil vegetarian meal is heavily dependent on rice, vegetables and lentil preparations such as _rasam_ and _sambar_ , but there are variations. They have influenced Kerala as well in their _kootu_ , _arachi vitta sambhar_[[185]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Indian_cuisine#cite_note-185) and _molagootals_ ([mulligatawny](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Mulligatawny "Mulligatawny") soup). 
As mentioned above, the Chettinad variety of food uses many strong spices, such as pepper, garlic, fennel seeds and onions. Tamil food tends to be spicy compared to other parts of India so there is a tradition of finishing the meal with [_dahi_](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Dahi_\(curd\) "Dahi \(curd\)") (yogurt) is considered a soothing end to the meal. 
Notably, [Tamil Brahmin](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Tamil_Brahmin "Tamil Brahmin") cuisine, the food of the [Iyers](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Iyer "Iyer") and [Iyengar](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Iyengar "Iyengar") community, is characterised by slightly different meal times and meal structures compared to other communities within the state. 
Historically vegetarian, the cuisine is known for its milder flavour and avoidance of onion and garlic (although this practice appears to be disappearing with time). 
After a light morning meal of [filter coffee](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Filter_coffee "Filter coffee") and different varieties of porridges (oatmeal and _janata kanji_ are immensely popular), the main meal of the day, lunch/brunch is usually at 11 am and typically follows a two-three course meal structure. Steamed rice is the main dish, and is always accompanied by a seasonally steamed/sauteed vegetable (_[poriyal](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Poriyal "Poriyal")_), and two or three types of tamarind stews, the most popular being [_sambhar_](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Sambar_\(dish\) "Sambar \(dish\)") and [_rasam_](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Rasam_\(dish\) "Rasam \(dish\)"). The meal typically ends with _[thair sadham](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Curd_rice "Curd rice")_ (rice with yogurt), usually served with pickled mangoes or lemons. 
_Tiffin_ is the second meal of the day and features several breakfast favourites such as _[idli](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Idli "Idli")_ , _[rava idli](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Rava_idli "Rava idli")_ , _[upma](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Upma "Upma")_ , _dosa_ varieties, and _[vada](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Vada_\(food\) "Vada \(food\)")_ , and is usually accompanied by _chai_. 
Dinner is the simplest meal of the day, typically involving leftovers from either lunch or _tiffin_. Fresh seasonal fruit consumed in the state include bananas, papaya, honeydew and cantaloupe melons, jackfruit, mangos, apples, [_kasturi_ oranges](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Calamansi "Calamansi"), pomegranates, and _nongu_ ([hearts of palm](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Hearts_of_palm "Hearts of palm")). 
### Telangana
Main article: [Telangana cuisine](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Telangana_cuisine "Telangana cuisine")
Further information: [Telugu cuisine](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Telugu_cuisine "Telugu cuisine") and [Hyderabadi cuisine](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Hyderabadi_cuisine "Hyderabadi cuisine")
[![](https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/4/4d/Chickenbiryani.JPG/250px-Chickenbiryani.JPG)](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/File:Chickenbiryani.JPG)_[Hyderabadi biryani](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Hyderabadi_biryani "Hyderabadi biryani")_ from Hyderabad
The cuisine of [Telangana](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Telangana "Telangana") consists of the [Telugu cuisine](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Telugu_cuisine "Telugu cuisine"), of Telangana's [Telugu people](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Telugu_people "Telugu people") as well as [Hyderabadi cuisine](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Hyderabadi_cuisine "Hyderabadi cuisine") (also known as [Nizami](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Nizam "Nizam") cuisine), of Telangana's [Hyderabadi Muslim community](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Hyderabadi_Muslims "Hyderabadi Muslims").[[186]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Indian_cuisine#cite_note-Dubey_and_Pat-186)[[187]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Indian_cuisine#cite_note-Pat-187)
[Hyderabadi food](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Hyderabadi_cuisine "Hyderabadi cuisine") is based heavily on non-vegetarian ingredients, while [Telugu food](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Telugu_cuisine "Telugu cuisine") is a mix of both vegetarian and non-vegetarian ingredients. Telugu food is rich in spices and chillies are abundantly used. The food also generally tends to be more on the tangy side with tamarind and lime juice both used liberally as souring agents. 
Rice is the staple food of Telugu people. Starch is consumed with a variety of curries and lentil [soups](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Soup "Soup") or [broths](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Broth "Broth").[[188]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Indian_cuisine#cite_note-Edelstein2011-188)[[189]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Indian_cuisine#cite_note-189) Vegetarian and non-vegetarian foods are both popular. 
Hyderabadi cuisine includes popular delicacies such as _[biryani](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Hyderabadi_Biryani "Hyderabadi Biryani")_ , _[haleem](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Hyderabadi_Haleem "Hyderabadi Haleem")_ , _[Baghara baingan](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Baghara_baingan "Baghara baingan")_ and _kheema,_ while Hyderabadi day-to-day dishes see some similarities to Telanganite Telugu food, with its use of tamarind, rice, and lentils, along with meat.[[188]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Indian_cuisine#cite_note-Edelstein2011-188) [_Dahi_](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Dahi_\(curd\) "Dahi \(curd\)") (yogurt) is a common addition to meals, as a way of tempering [spiciness](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Piquance "Piquance").[[190]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Indian_cuisine#cite_note-Dum_Pukht_cooking-190)
### Tripura
Main article: [Tripuri cuisine](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Tripuri_cuisine "Tripuri cuisine")
[![](https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/7/72/Tripuri_cusine.jpg/250px-Tripuri_cusine.jpg)](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/File:Tripuri_cusine.jpg)A Tripuri _[thali](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Thali "Thali")_
The [Tripuri people](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Tripuri_people "Tripuri people") are the original inhabitants of the state of [Tripura](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Tripura "Tripura") in northeast India. Today, they comprise the communities of [Tipra](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Tripuri_people "Tripuri people"), [Reang](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Reang "Reang"), [Jamatia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Jamatia "Jamatia"), [Noatia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Noatia "Noatia"), and [Uchoi](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Uchoi "Uchoi"), among others. The Tripuri are non-vegetarian,[[191]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Indian_cuisine#cite_note-Kumar2000-191) although they have a minority of [Vaishnavite](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Vaishnavism "Vaishnavism") vegetarians.[[192]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Indian_cuisine#cite_note-BahadurChib1977-192)
The major ingredients of Tripuri cuisine include vegetables, herbs, pork, chicken, mutton, fishes, turtle, shrimps, crabs, freshwater mussels, periwinkles, edible freshwater snails and frogs. 
### Uttar Pradesh
Main article: [Cuisine of Uttar Pradesh](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Cuisine_of_Uttar_Pradesh "Cuisine of Uttar Pradesh")
Further information: [Awadhi cuisine](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Awadhi_cuisine "Awadhi cuisine") and [Bhojpuri cuisine](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Bhojpuri_cuisine "Bhojpuri cuisine")
[![](https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/4/49/Vegetarian_Curry.jpeg/250px-Vegetarian_Curry.jpeg)](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/File:Vegetarian_Curry.jpeg)Uttar Pradeshi _[thali](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Thali "Thali")_ (platter) with _[naan](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Naan "Naan")_ , _[rice](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Rice "Rice")_ , _[daal](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Dal "Dal")_ , _[raita](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Raita "Raita")_ , _[shahi paneer](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Shahi_paneer "Shahi paneer")_ , and [salad](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Salad "Salad")
Traditionally, [Uttar Pradeshi](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Uttar_Pradesh "Uttar Pradesh") cuisine consists of [Awadhi](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Awadhi_cuisine "Awadhi cuisine"), [Bhojpuri](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Bhojpuri_cuisine "Bhojpuri cuisine"), and [Mughlai cuisine](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Mughlai_cuisine "Mughlai cuisine"),[[193]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Indian_cuisine#cite_note-Dubey20111-193) though a vast majority[_[citation needed](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")_] of the state is vegetarian, preferring _dal_ , _roti_ , _sabzi_ , and rice. _[Pooris](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Poori "Poori")_ and _[kachoris](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Kachori "Kachori")_ are eaten on special occasions. 
_[Chaat](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Chaat "Chaat")_ , _[samosa](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Samosa "Samosa")_ , and _[pakora](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Pakora "Pakora")_ , among the most popular snacks in India, originate from Uttar Pradesh.[[194]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Indian_cuisine#cite_note-Kapoor2002A-194)[[195]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Indian_cuisine#cite_note-Lamba2002-195)
Well-known dishes include _[kebabs](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Kebab "Kebab")_ , _[dum biryani](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Biryani "Biryani")_ , and various [mutton](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Mutton "Mutton") recipes. _[Sheer qorma](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Sheer_khurma "Sheer khurma")_ , _[ghevar](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ghevar "Ghevar")_ , _[gulab jamun](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Gulab_jamun "Gulab jamun")_ , _[kheer](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Kheer "Kheer")_ , and _[ras malai](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ras_malai "Ras malai")_ are some of the popular desserts in this region. 
[Awadhi cuisine](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Awadhi_cuisine "Awadhi cuisine") ([Hindi](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Hindi_language "Hindi language"): अवधी खाना) is from the city of [Lucknow](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Lucknow "Lucknow"), which is the capital of the state of [Uttar Pradesh](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Uttar_Pradesh "Uttar Pradesh") in [Central-South Asia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Central_Asia "Central Asia") and [North India](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/North_India "North India"), and the cooking patterns of the city are similar to those of Central Asia, the Middle East, and other parts of North India. The cuisine consists of both vegetarian and non-vegetarian dishes. 
[Awadh](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Awadh "Awadh") has been greatly influenced by [Mughal cooking techniques](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Mughlai_cuisine "Mughlai cuisine"), and the cuisine of Lucknow bears similarities to those of [Central Asia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Central_Asian_Cuisine "Central Asian Cuisine"), [Kashmir](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Kashmir "Kashmir"), [Punjab](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Punjab_region "Punjab region") and [Hyderabad](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Hyderabad,_India "Hyderabad, India"). The city is also known for its [Nawabi](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Nawab "Nawab") foods.[[196]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Indian_cuisine#cite_note-books.google.nl-196) The _bawarchis_ and _rakabdars_ of Awadh gave birth to the [_dum_ style of cooking](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Dum_Pukht "Dum Pukht") or the art of cooking over a slow fire, which has become synonymous with Lucknow today.[[197]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Indian_cuisine#cite_note-197) Their spread consisted of elaborate dishes like _[kebabs](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Kebab "Kebab")_ , _[kormas](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Korma "Korma")_ , _[biryani](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Biryani "Biryani")_ , _kaliya_ , [_nahari-kulchas_](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Kulcha "Kulcha"), _[zarda](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zarda_\(food\) "Zarda \(food\)")_ , _[sheermal](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Sheermal "Sheermal")_ , [_roomali rotis_](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Rumali_roti "Rumali roti"), and [_warqi parathas_](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Paratha "Paratha"). 
The richness of Awadh cuisine lies not only in the variety of cuisine but also in the ingredients used like [mutton](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Mutton "Mutton"), _[paneer](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Paneer "Paneer")_ , and rich spices, including [cardamom](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Cardamom "Cardamom") and [saffron](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Saffron "Saffron"). 
[Mughlai cuisine](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Mughlai_cuisine "Mughlai cuisine") is a style of cooking developed in the Indian subcontinent by the [imperial](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/imperial "wikt:imperial") kitchens of the [Mughal Empire](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Mughal_Empire "Mughal Empire"). It represents the cooking styles used in [North India](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/North_India "North India") (especially [Uttar Pradesh](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Uttar_Pradesh "Uttar Pradesh")). 
The cuisine is strongly influenced by [Central Asian cuisine](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Central_Asian_cuisine "Central Asian cuisine"), the region where the [Chagatai-Turkic](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Chagatai_Khanate "Chagatai Khanate") Mughal rulers originally hailed from, and has strongly influenced the regional cuisines of [Kashmir](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Kashmiri_cuisine "Kashmiri cuisine") and the [Punjab](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Punjabi_cuisine "Punjabi cuisine") region.[[198]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Indian_cuisine#cite_note-198)[[196]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Indian_cuisine#cite_note-books.google.nl-196)
The tastes of Mughlai cuisine vary from extremely mild to spicy, and is often associated with a distinctive [aroma](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Aroma "Aroma") and the taste of ground and whole spices. 
A Mughlai course is an elaborate [buffet](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Buffet "Buffet") of main course dishes with a variety of [accompaniments](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Side_dish "Side dish").[[199]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Indian_cuisine#cite_note-199)
### Uttarakhand
Main article: [Kumauni cuisine](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Kumauni_cuisine "Kumauni cuisine")
[![](https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/8/8d/Saag_Paneer.jpg/250px-Saag_Paneer.jpg)](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/File:Saag_Paneer.jpg)_[Saag](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Saag "Saag")_ , a popular Kumauni dish from Uttarakhand, made from green vegetables such as [spinach](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Spinach "Spinach") and [fenugreek](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Fenugreek "Fenugreek")
Food from [Uttrakhand](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Uttrakhand "Uttrakhand") is known to be healthy and wholesome to suit the high-energy necessities of the cold, mountainous region. It is a high-protein diet that makes heavy use of [pulses](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Pulses "Pulses") and vegetables. Traditionally, it is cooked over wood or charcoal fire mostly using iron utensils. 
While making use of condiments such as _jeera_ , _haldi_ and _rai_ common in other Indian cuisines, Uttarakhand cuisine also use exotic ingredients such as _jambu_ , _timmer_ , _ghandhraini_ and _[bhangira](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Perilla_frutescens#India "Perilla frutescens")_. 
Although the people in Uttarakhand also make dishes common in other parts of northern India, several preparations are unique to Uttarakhand such as _rus_ , _chudkani_ ,[[200]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Indian_cuisine#cite_note-200) _dubuk_ , _chadanji_ ,[[201]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Indian_cuisine#cite_note-201) _jholi_ , _kapa_ , and more. 
Among dressed salads and sauces, _kheere ka raita_ ,[[202]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Indian_cuisine#cite_note-202) _nimbu mooli ka raita_ ,[[203]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Indian_cuisine#cite_note-203) _daarim ki khatai_ and _aam ka fajitha_[[204]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Indian_cuisine#cite_note-204) are also popular. 
The cuisine mainly consists of food from two different sub-regions, [Garhwal](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Garhwal_division "Garhwal division") and [Kumaon](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Kumaon_division "Kumaon division"), though their basic ingredients are the same.[[205]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Indian_cuisine#cite_note-DewanSingh2001-205) Both Kumaoni and Garhwali styles make liberal use of _ghee_ , lentils or pulses, vegetables and _bhaat_ (rice). They also use _badi_ (sun-dried _[urad dal](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Vigna_mungo#Cooking "Vigna mungo")_ balls) and _mungodi_ (sun-dried _[moong dal](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Mung_bean#South_Asia "Mung bean")_ balls) as substitutes for vegetables at times. 
During festivals and other celebrations, the people of Uttarakhand prepare special refreshments which include both salty preparations such as _bada_ and sweet preparations such as _pua_ and _singal_. Uttarakhand also has several sweets (_[mithai](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Mithai_\(confectionery\) "Mithai \(confectionery\)")_) such as _[singodi](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Singori "Singori")_ , _[bal-mithai](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Bal_Mithai "Bal Mithai")_ , and _malai laddu_ ,[[206]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Indian_cuisine#cite_note-206) native to its traditions.
